name: Seawolves Soak City
description:
Join Student Engagement and Activities' Seawolves Soak City on Friday 8/29 at Staller Steps from 4-7 PM with numerous clubs!

Come by and enjoy water slides, obstacle courses, water guns, balloon fights, smoothies, & much more!

This event is open to all SBU students and guests and is free of charge! This event is open to all students. Come prepared with sunscreen, change of clothing, etc.

Save time & fill out the waiver now!

Day of the Event: Upon arriving at the event, staff will ask you for your event pass to scan. This can be found in the CORQ app under the drop-down tab once you sign-in to CORQ using your Stony Brook NetID. This is a contactless check-in system.

 

For accessibility needs please contact sea_programsandtraditions@stonybrook.edu ***RSVPing does not guarantee admittance if the event is full, it just reminds you of the event***

name: Spooky Brook 2025
description:
🎮 🎃 WELCOME TO YOUR NIGHT SHIFT AT SPOOKY BROOK 🎃

The cameras flicker. The power’s low. You’ve clocked in for one night: October 25, 7–11 PM and your goal is simple: survive the Spooky Brook shift.

Hosted by USG’s Student Activities Board (SAB) in collaboration with Student Engagement and Activities (SEA), this Halloween takeover transforms the Student Activities Center (SAC) into a spooky area.

🕹️ ACTIVITIES INCLUDE:
✨ Magic & Illusion Show
👻 Costume Contest- Sign up here!
🎯 Bingo
🧩 Escape Room
🧍‍♂️ Caricature Artists
🎨 Club Tabling
☕ Bar & Chill Zone
🧸 Stuff-A-Plush-sold out
🍬 Trick-or-Treat Station- sold out
🎥 Five Nights at Freddy’s Watch Party

🩸 Funded by your Student Activity Fee

For any strange occurrences, contact 📧 usg_studentlife@stonybrook.edu

☎️ 1-800-USGXSEA — ODD NOTES OUT.

They say the last guard heard whispers in the vents.

Click here to see all activities happening day of!

You were warned about the odd notes. 👁️

name: Red Day
description:
Every Friday at Stony Brook University is RED HOT FRIDAY! 

Join us for the first Red Hot Friday of the semester for games, food, giveaways and prizes at the Administration Building Fountain from 12-2pm! 

Wear your red to show your Stony Brook SPIRIT! 

This event is open to all SBU students and guests and is free of charge! 

For accessibility needs please contact studentengagement@stonybrook.edu.

name: Intro to ASA: Matcha and Meet!
description: “Intro to ASA”  is an event that serves to promote Asian American culture in Stony Brook by creating a familial atmosphere. ASA’s dedication to introduce and bring awareness to the Asian community is manifested through the progress of past events. Not only will the event display the diversity of the community, but it also showcases the various personalities of ASA’s members. Through presentations and games, Intro to ASA allows participants to get to know the members on a deeper level, segueing into the opportunity to sign up for an interview for a cabinet position.

Join us for “Sips & Strokes,” where you'll sip on custom mocktails while painting your very own masterpiece. You do not need any experience. It's the perfect way to unwind, meet people from the community, and make something memorable together. All supplies (and good vibes) will be provided!

description: BTC's first social of the fall semester. A chance for previous and new genbod members to meet the cabinet and EBoard of the Baan Thai Club. New representatives and officers will also be announced at this event. Homemade tea will be served throughout this event.

name: Tune in with CB
description: Curious about CB and looking to connect with Chinese culture? Come vibe with us, play games, and get to know CB! Grab a goodie bag of iconic Chinese snacks and compete for prizes. Don’t miss out on the fun!

name: Heat & Heat: Fire Safety and Prevention
description:
Fire prevention at Stony Brook University is a year-round effort by Fire and Emergency Services. During the month of September, we conduct numerous fire safety lectures, fire extinguisher training, and live-burn demonstrations in each of the residential communities and host an annual fire and safety expo on the academic mall. 

Join us and the first 300 participants will get a voucher for food and refreshment for the event.

name: Heat & Heat: Fire Safety and Prevention
description:
Fire prevention at Stony Brook University is a year-round effort by Fire and Emergency Services. During the month of September, we conduct numerous fire safety lectures, fire extinguisher training, and live-burn demonstrations in each of the residential communities and host an annual fire and safety expo on the academic mall. 

Join us and the first 300 participants will get a voucher for food and refreshment for the event.

name: Drop-In Support Space
description:
A weekly drop-in discussion group for students with disabilities to share their unique experiences and develop community and support to navigate the challenges as college students. Students are encouraged to use the space and time to build social connections and learn from one another.

 

Light refreshments and games will be provided. NO REGISTRATION REQUIRED, JUST DROP IN!

name: Taking On The Semseter!
description: • Join RA John in the Langmuir Hall Lounge for Taking on the Semester, a workshop designed to help students plan, organize, and succeed academically and personally this semester. The session will provide practical strategies for time management, study habits, and goal-setting to set you up for success. Light refreshments will be served, and residents will have the opportunity to connect with peers while gaining useful tools for the semester ahead.

name: HSA Bazaar
description: Step into the vibrant world of the Himalayas right on campus! Experience the lively, colorful vibe of a Himalayan marketplace with games, live music, and delicious Himalayan snacks and street food. Come hang out, have fun, and dive into the colorful traditions of the Himalayas.

name: Tea and Talk
description: Come ready to sip and speak! Enjoy some hot tea, fresh brewed from our samovar! No prior knowledge of Russian is needed, all are welcome.

name: ✨ Diamond Paint & Sip Night ✨
description: Come get a free diamond painting kit and sip some juice while you relax and hang out with your neighbors! 🎨🥤 Join RA Arafat and RA Shoha for a fun evening.

name: Sunrise GBM
description:
Sunrise SBU GBMs are a weekly space where student climate activists, organizers, and new members come together to connect, plan, and build collective power to fight for climate justice at Stony Brook University, in New York State, and across the nation.

Sunrise SBU is a hub of the Sunrise Movement, a youth-led movement to stop climate change and create millions of good jobs through a Green New Deal. As a hub, we work to empower students to take action through grassroots organizing, political education, and coalition-building.

name: Roundnet Paint Night
description: Come join SBU Roundnet club for a paint night!

name: Pre-Health APAMSA: Non-Traditional Healthcare Careers
description: Join us for our next GBM as we highlight non-traditional healthcare careers! From patient care technicians (PCTs) and certified nursing assistants (CNAs) to roles in public health, policy, and biotech, there are many meaningful paths beyond the traditional pre-med track. Come learn how these careers make an impact—especially within the AAPI community—and explore new opportunities for your future.
categories: Asian Pacific Islander Desi American Heritage Month

name: Unlocking Campus & Friends: CSTEP Opening GBM!!
description:
Come join CSTEP in celebrating the start of the Fall 2025 semester! Get to know fellow CSTEPers, Advisory Board members, and find out about future CSTEP events. We will be going over on campus resources, playing fun games, having a raffle (with prizes 🙂) and eating yummy food! Don’t miss out on our first event of the semester!  

 

RSVP HERE by Friday, September 12th!!

name: SBU Comp Prog Weekly Meeting
description:
Every week, we meet on Tuesday at 7pm in the Old CS Building Room 2114. At each meeting, we hold a practice contest on vjudge.com, and we have free pizza. 

Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club is a place for anyone interested in algorithms and programming. We offer lectures on algorithmic problems solving, host speakers to discuss algorithms, and provide training sessions for our ICPC teams.
The weekly contest takes place on vjudge.com, where participants are presented with a set of problems to solve within specified time and memory constraints. Solutions can be submitted in various programming languages, including C, C++, Java, and Python, among others. All students from SBU are encouraged to participate. We usually have a collaborative environment where everyone can seek assistance from each other. If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!

name: Pizza' The Pie
description:
FREE PIZZA!!! FREE PIZZAAAA.

 

The first 40 minutes will be spent playing a trivia game modeled after Trivial Pursuit Live!, with rounds including Quickstarter, Grab Bag, Switchagories, Close Call, and Rapid Fire. Questions will cover a range of STEM topics such as biology, chemistry, physics, technology, and biomedical engineering. Participants earn wedges by answering questions correctly, and each wedge corresponds to slices of pizza, which will be served during the final 20 minutes of the event based on how well the team performs. The winning team will win a set of prizes as well!

description:
Commuter Student Services and Off-Campus Living presents Bus Stop Bites! Each month, we will be hosting two Bus Stop Bites event to feed our commuter students. Join us for a grab-and-go lunch as you head home for the day or come in for classes!

Supplies are limited and will be served on a first come, first serve basis.

Come get to know Stony Brook alumnus and author Ross Barkan as he shares insights into his journey as a writer and explores the value of journalism, as reflected in his work.

Following the discussion during Homecoming, enjoy light refreshments and the opportunity to connect with fellow alumni, current students, faculty and Ross himself.

name: 11/19 Wicked 2: For Good: Pre- Screening!
description:
✨ Join Us for a Wickedly Wonderful Night! ✨

Join us on Wednesday, November 19th for a magical trip to Island 16 Cinema de Lux for an exclusive pre-screening of 🌟 Wicked: The Movie Part 2 🌟 

see it before it officially hits theaters!

🎟️ Your Ticket Includes:
🏰 Round-trip travel to the theater
🎬 A private showing of Wicked: The Movie
🍫 Snacks to keep you fueled for the adventure

🍿 Special Treats:
The first 50 students to purchase tickets will receive a FREE Medium Popcorn & Soft Drink Combo Voucher!
After that, vouchers are available on a first-come, first-served basis — so grab your ticket before they fly away on a broomstick! ✨

🚌 Travel Details:
🕕 The bus (our enchanted carriage for the evening 🏰) departs from the Stony Brook Union at 6:00 PM
🎥 The movie runs approximately 2 hours and 40 minutes
🚌 The bus will return to campus right after the credits roll

🚍 Transportation Policy:
All students must use the provided transportation to and from the theater unless otherwise approved by the trip chaperone on the day of the event.

⚠️ Failure to follow this policy may result in suspension from future off-campus trips.

💰 Ticket Prices:
🎟 Undergraduate students: $10
🎟 Graduate students: $15

✨✨
🎫 TICKETS GO ON SALE MONDAY, NOVEMBER 10TH AT 9:00 AM!
DON’T MISS YOUR CHANCE TO SNAG A SPOT BEFORE THEY FLY AWAY ON A BROOMSTICK! 🧹
✨✨

CLICK HERE TO BUY TICKETS

🎟️ Tickets are non-transferable and non-refundable.

📧 For questions (or if you’re feeling a little green with excitement 💚), email BeyondtheBrook@stonybrook.edu.

name: Fall Fair

If you loved Wolfieland but hated the lines and love a farmer’s market but don’t have any way of getting to one, come join BWA for an afternoon of games, like cornhole and ring toss, snacks like cupcakes and popcorn, the chance to pie a BWA member, and even better, the opportunity to win baskets such as fall-themed body care and a midterm survival kit.

name: SBU Homecoming Banner
description:
🎨 Homecoming Banner Painting Night 🖌️
📅 Date: October 8, 2025
🕕 Time: 6:00 PM – 7:00 PM
📍 Location: Hand Classroom, Stony Brook University

Get ready to unleash your creativity and show your Seawolf spirit at our Homecoming Banner Painting Night! Join us for a fun and relaxed evening filled with painting, good music, and FREE pizza.

Whether you're an artist or just here for the vibes, this is your chance to help bring Homecoming to life in a colorful, creative environment. All supplies will be provided—just bring yourself and your school spirit!

Let’s make something amazing together—one brushstroke at a time! 🎉

name: PING PONG TOURNAMENT
description:
Resident Ping Pong Tournament! This event is designed for residents of all skill levels, whether you’re a seasoned player or trying ping pong for the first time. Come join us for an afternoon of friendly competition, community bonding, and lots of fun.

The tournament will take place in Chapin MPR, with multiple tables set up to ensure everyone gets plenty of time to play. Matches will be fast-paced and exciting, and winners will advance through the brackets until we crown the ultimate ping pong champion. Don’t worry if you’re new to the game—we’ll have volunteers on hand to explain the rules and give tips to help you improve your serve and returns.

Beyond the competition, this is a great opportunity to meet fellow residents, make new friends, and enjoy a lively, energetic atmosphere. Snacks and refreshments will be provided to keep your energy up while you battle it out for bragging rights and prizes.

description:
Spanish Bilingual Mass
Sunday, October 26th, 2025 | 5:00 PM | SB Union Ballroom (2nd level)

Join us for a special Spanish-English Bilingual Mass in honor of Hispanic Heritage Month. All members of the Stony Brook University community are welcome to attend. Please help us welcome our Special Guest Celebrant: Bishop John Oliver Barres of the Diocese of Rockville Center (Long Island, NY).

Stay after the Mass for a Community Social featuring Latin American refreshments—an opportunity to connect, celebrate, and enjoy good company.

Sponsored by: Hispanic Heritage Month Committee and Catholic Campus Ministry

name: AAPI Mentorship Mentee Mixer
description: Join us for a fun and welcoming evening to kick off the AAPI Mentorship program! Meet fellow mentees, and build community through interactive games, good conversation, and great food. This event isn't just for AAPI mentees though. Come learn about the AAPI mentorship and what we're about, and also to meet some of our mentees! We’ll have pizza, prizes, and plenty of laughs as we get to know each other and celebrate the start of the semester together. There will be a cap of 50 people so make sure to get here early! Whether you’re here to make new friends, find guidance, or just enjoy some good vibes, this is the perfect chance to get involved!

name: CSA's Fall Relax-a-thon!
description:
Take a deep breath and unwind with the Commuter Student Association’s signature Relax-a-thon, your favorite mid-semester de-stress event!✨

Join us for an afternoon filled with free food, refreshments, and desserts, plus creative and cozy activities including Stuff-A-Plushies, games, a photo booth, and more! Whether you’re looking to relax between classes or just spend time with friends, Relax-a-thon is the perfect way to recharge before finals!

name: Jeopardy Night: Around the World Edition
description: Come join RA Tolani and RA Nia in Whitman Hall main lounge for a fun night of cultural themed jeopardy! Win free prizes, learn about different cultures and enjoy snacks. Be there November 3rd, 2025, Monday at 7pm. For any questions or accessibility, please contact @roth_areaoffice@stonybrook.edu .

name: GradConnect Series
description:
We are excited to invite graduate students to the October GradConnect Series, organized by the Graduate School.

 

This new monthly series will partner with on-campus and off-campus resources to provide students with direct opportunities to learn about available services and connect. The event will also be a platform for graduate students to interact with each other, share experiences, and build a supportive community.

 

For the October GradConnect Series event, we are delighted that the following campus departments and resources are joining to support and connect with graduate students.

 

  • Recreation and Wellness 
  • Student Accessibility and Support Center (SASC) 
  • Student Health Services (SHS)

 

 

Light refreshments will be provided.

 

 Plus, attend at least three of the four Fall 2025 events in the GradConnect Series to be entered into our Grand Raffle Prize drawing in December!  

 

Looking forward to seeing you at the GradConnect Series event.

name: 2025 Fall CGSA GBM
description: The Chinese Graduate Students Association (CGSA) aims to help Chinese graduate students at Stony Brook University relieve their possible feelings of isolation and find their community. We’ll be hosting a fun night filled with board games, food, and a chance to connect with fellow graduate students. Whether you’re a returning member or new to the community, everyone is welcome to join the fun!

name: First Gen Cafe
description:
Come chill with us at FirstGen Café—a cozy, low-key space to grab a coffee and connect with other first-gen folk. No agenda, no stress. Just good vibes, shared experiences, and a chance to build community with people who understand your journey.

Friday, Nov. 7 | 6 PM- 8 PM |UNITI Cultural Center- SB Union 113

name: Qawwali Night
description: The Pakistani Student Association will be hosting a Qawwali Night to celebrate the rich musical and spiritual traditions of South Asia. This event will feature soulful Qawwali performances, providing students with an opportunity to enjoy live music, cultural appreciation, and community connection.

name: Bagel Bash!
description: Join Stony Brook Hillel's weekly Bagel Bash! Every Wednesday between 12:30 PM and 2:00PM Hillel is happy to host a delicious. and fresh Bagel Brunch, bring your friends and enjoy a delicious time!

name: Tea Across the Globe!!
description:
Celebrate Culture. Share Stories. Sip Together.

Take a trip around the world—one cup of tea at a time! 🌎✨
Join us for Tea Around the World, a cozy cultural experience where we’ll sample teas from different countries and explore the stories, traditions, and people behind them. From Japanese matcha and Moroccan mint to Indian chai and British breakfast blends, each sip will transport you to a different part of the globe.

Residents and international students will share fun cultural facts, personal experiences, and even a few musical moments from their home countries. This event is all about connection — discovering the beauty of diversity, learning something new, and appreciating how one simple drink can bring people together across borders.

Enjoy light snacks, music from around the world, and great company in a warm, welcoming atmosphere. Whether you’re a tea lover or just looking for meaningful conversations, you won’t want to miss this evening of flavor, friendship, and cultural appreciation.

name: Wagner Hall Council GBM!
description: Want to know what's going on in Wagner Hall? Have some awesome suggestions for future events or changes? Then you should come to our GBMs for Wagner Hall Council, weekly on Thursday @8:30pm. We'll be discussing everything the Hall Council has planned for the future and taking in any ideas and feedback from anyone!

name: PCC Pizza Night
description: Come join PCC for a relaxing night with pizza. Take a breather from midterms for a night, bring a friend!

name: Soup For The Soul
description: Come to RSST in Schomburg Commons to learn and discuss mental health stigmas and the boundaries that hold students back from getting support and aid. Come try soups from around the world and build your own take home soup.

name: Ecology and Evolutions Society Club: Integrative Taxonomy of Calcareous Sponges
description:
Sponges are thought to be one of the most peculiar animals on this planet.

Calcareous sponges are the most obscure and unique sponges in a already unique species group. Raisa Rizzieri has dedicated much of her time studying and learning about these peculiar creatures and now she's ready to share her intriguing discoveries regarding the taxonomy of Calcareous Sponges. She will also be sharing valuable career advise for those looking to gain a research career or seeking higher education in Ecology and Evolution.

Date: November 18th | 4:30PM - 5:30PM

Location: Life Sciences | Conference Rm 650

There will be Free Food, Fun Games, and lots of Valuable Advise!

name: NAACP Trivia Night
description:
Join us for an evening of friendly competition at our NAACP Trivia Night! Learn and discuss more about current events, with special focus on the recent government shutdown. This interactive event blends fun and education, providing a safe space to discuss personal feelings surrounding current events, how they affect our community, and ways we can get involved to spark change.

Pizza and refreshments will be provided!

name: Win a Lego Set! Compete for the Best Spagetti Tower!
description: Do you like building but not an engineer? Do you like legos? Do you want to make friends? Come to our spaghetti and marshmellow tower night! Students will be randomly organized into teams and will have a set of spaghetti and marshmallows in attempt to make the tallest tower! Winners will receive lego products and the title of tower building expert!

description:

Don’t miss this opportunity to refine your professional presence and enjoy a great meal!
RSVP by November 6 using the QR code!

name: Gingerbread House Decorating Contest
description:
Get ready to build, decorate, and snack your way to glory! Join us for the Yang Hall Gingerbread Decorating Contest as we celebrate nearing the end of the semester and the frosty weather outside ❄️. Teams of 4–6 people will compete to create a cookie masterpiece 🎄🏠. We also have paper snowflake making, trivia, and a hot cocoa bar for everyone to enjoy!

Seating is limited and capped at 50 participants, so grab your friends and sign up fast! All supplies provided and all skill levels welcome🍬✨ Everyone is welcome to RSVP, so don’t miss out!

Date: 12/02/25

Time: 7-9 PM 

Location: Schomburg Commons

name: Journal at Stony's Self-Care Night
description: Take some time to destress and have a cozy evening with Journal at Stony! We will provide guided journaling prompts, soft music, tea, and face masks to take! Bring a journal you intend to use at club meetings and feel free to bring a friend or two!

name: Take To Cafe Con LASO
description:
🌙 Join me for a night out with LASO! 💫
I’ll be taking residents to Café con LASO on Friday, November 14th, 2025, at the Bauman Center — one of the most special nights of the semester! Come enjoy an evening filled with música, poesía, arte, y cultura, and celebrate nuestras raíces y nuestros sueños together 🕯️

✨ Meet-up time: 6:10 PM in the James lobby (doors open at 6:30 PM!)
👗 Dress code: Semi-formal — look your best and get ready for photos!
🍽️ Dinner will be provided, plus chances to win amazing prizes 🤩
📸 Bonus: The first 100 guests will receive a free Polaroid picture to capture the night!

Let’s enjoy a cozy night of community, culture, and connection under the stars ✨
Trust me, you won’t want to miss this! 💛

Contact: lizbeth.martineztapia@stonybrook.edu if any questions arise!

description:
✨ Hanukkah Brunch with JSA ✨

Settle into the season with a cozy Hanukkah Brunch hosted by JSA on Tuesday, December 2nd, from 1–3pm in the Union Ballroom. Get a taste of Jachnun and other Mizrahi dishes served in a warm, welcoming holiday setting. As we begin the Festival of Lights, we invite you to share good food, good company, and the simple joy of celebrating Hanukkah together. ❄️🕎✨

📍Union Ballroom

🗓️ Tuesday, December 2nd

⏰ 1-3pm

name: E-0 and D-1 Take To: Starbucks
description: Come join RA John of floor E-0 and RA James of floor D-1 to a trip to the library Starbucks. We will get some coffee and take a breather from classes for a bit. You can either meet us there or walk with us to Starbucks from the Benedict North lobby. Whether you want to bring a laptop to do some work, or you just want a little break, come down with us for a chill time. First 15 people to RSVP get a free drink.

name: Are You Smarter Than a 5th Grader?
description:
Join a collab between the Kelly RA Christian Akaeze and West RAs Shalom Obimba, James Gill, and Kevin Lam for a night of fun, competition, and nostalgia with Are You Smarter Than a 5th Grader? Test your knowledge on subjects you thought you mastered back in elementary school math, science, history, and more! Work in teams or go solo as you face off against your friends to see who really remembers their 5th-grade facts.

Snacks and prizes will be provided, so bring your brainpower and school spirit for a night full of laughs and learning!

name: PASA's Christmas Party
description:
Join the Polish American Student Association for our annual Christmas Party and celebrate the holidays with delicious traditional Polish food and festive cheer! Enjoy a cozy evening filled with good company, music, and authentic dishes, including:

🥟 Pierogi with sauerkraut
🥟 Pierogi with cheese and potato
🥟 Pierogi with meat
🌭 Kielbasa (Polish sausage)
🥒 Jar of pickles
🍞 Loaf of bread
🥤 Tymbark and orange soda

Come hungry, bring your holiday spirit, and experience the warmth of a true Polish Christmas! Everyone is welcome — don’t miss out on one of PASA’s favorite end-of-year traditions!

name: Felt The Joy
description: JOIN SASE FOR OUR LAST EVENT OF THE SEMESTER CREATE UR OWN KEYCHAINS

name: Midterm gateway: Make your own ramen & bubble tea
description:
Take a break from the midterm chaos and treat yourself! 🍜🧋✨
Join Pal Nusrat, Vamsi & Kirsten for a cozy make-your-own ramen and bubble tea hangout!

Build everything your way:
• Ramen: choose your ramen & add your toppings 🍲
• Bubble Tea: pick your tea flavor & boba, toppings 😋

It’s 100% free, fun, and perfect for this chilly weather! ❄️💛

Note: Supplies are limited — RSVP ASAP and grab your warm ramen + refreshing boba before we run out! 🏃‍♂️🏃‍♀️💨

name: Russian Corner and Hillel New Year’s Celebration
description:
Join Russian Corner and Hillel for Noviy God, the traditional Russian New Year celebration, on December 2 at 7 PM in the SAC Shore Club!

Step into the magic of a Russian winter evening filled with delicious food, festive traditions, and joyful celebration. Enjoy classic New Year’s dishes, lively music, and games that capture the warmth and spirit of this beloved holiday.

Experience Russian New Year customs — from Ded Moroz (Grandfather Frost) and Snegurochka (the Snow Maiden)to the sparkle of the New Year tree and toasts for good fortune in the coming year. Whether you’re familiar with Russian culture or just looking for a fun and welcoming celebration, everyone is invited to join the festivities!

name: Friendsgiving with TASA
description:
Join the Turkish-American Student Association for a cozy Friendsgiving celebration! Come enjoy a warm, welcoming evening filled with good food, great conversations, and the comfort of community before the holiday break.

This will be a potluck-style gathering, bring a dish, snack, or dessert to share with friends! Whether it's something homemade, store-bought, or a favorite cultural dish, everything is welcome.

📅 Date: Tuesday, November 25th
⏰ Time: 5:00–7:00 PM
📍 Location: SAC Shore Club
📝 RSVP Required: This event is RSVP-only to help us prepare accordingly!

Expect fall vibes, music, fun, and a relaxing evening to connect and unwind. We can’t wait to celebrate with you! 🍗🥧❤️

name: Chinese Food Shabbat
description:
Chinese Food Shabbat: The Ultimate Friday Night Mashup 🍜✨

Forget everything you thought you knew about Shabbat dinner… because this week, we’re serving up Chinese food, Hillel-style! Think the comfort of Shabbat vibes + the joy of scallion pancakes, lo mein, dumplings, and all your takeout favorites — all in one delicious, communal, vibey night.

Join us for a laid-back, laughter-filled evening with great food, great friends, and zero prep dishes. Whether you’re craving sesame noodles or just looking for a warm space to unwind after a long week, this is the perfect spot.

Come hungry. Leave happy. Bring your friends.
Shabbat shalom… but make it delicious 😌🥡

name: FCC Grow a Rice Paddy
description: Welcome to Grow a Rice Paddy! Learn about the origins of rice and its role in Fujianese history, with a theme similar to the popular Roblox game, Grow a Garden. You can explore interactive games, enjoy delicious food, and participate in engaging activities that highlight rice. The event also features a selection of food and drinks that reflect authentic flavors. Come to Grow a Rice Paddy and have a great time with the Fujianese Culture Club!

name: PHSRG Monthly Member Meeting
description: Upcoming Meeting: we have scheduled our second monthly meeting for Wednesday, October 22nd at 4:30 pm in HSC 2-272AB and a virtual option is also included. The details have been posted under the "Monthly Meetings" module. We will have a panel of current/former PhD students and graduate students from the Program in Public Health to share their research journeys. This will be followed by a quick brainstorming session in small groups to identify research interests and identify sub-committees for specific topics. Lastly, we will review once more the executive board positions available that need to be fulfilled and review the agenda for our next meeting in early November.

name: Kremas and Chill: Finals Relief Edition
description:
Join us for Kremas & Chill, a cozy end-of-semester gathering filled with good vibes, Haitian flavor, and stress-free fun. Come sip on some delicious non-alcoholic Kremas (Haiti’s signature holiday drink 🥥), snack on your favorite treats, and relax with friends before the semester wraps up.

We'll have:

- A Kremas bar with various toppings

- Holiday movie and chill corner 🎬

- And an affirmations station to share some positive words and well wishes for the end of our semester! 💫

Let's end the semester on a sweet note! ❤️🇭🇹

name: CASB Presents: Winter Wondershop: Polar Express Edition
description:
❄️ Winter Wondershop: Polar Express Edition

Date: December 8, 2025
Time: 7:00 PM – 10:00 PM
Location: LDS Bauman Center

Embark on a magical holiday adventure at Winter Wondershop: Polar Express Edition! Inspired by the warmth and wonder of the classic winter tale, this festive evening invites you to step into a cozy world of creativity, treats, and seasonal cheer. Enjoy hands-on holiday crafts, themed activities, and immersive winter-inspired stations perfect for getting into the spirit of the season. Sip on hot cocoa, make your own take-home keepsakes, and unwind in a welcoming community space designed to spark joy and connection.

All aboard for an unforgettable night of winter magic—your Polar Express journey awaits!

name: Dragon Boat Race Festival 2025
description:
Paddlers wanted! 

Stony Brook University & Stony Brook Medicine employees and students are invited to participate in the Annual Port Jefferson Dragon Boat Race Festival on Saturday, September 13, 2025. This event is hosted by the Port Jefferson Chamber of Commerce and Stony Brook University is happy to be invited the Festival for the last 11 years.

Complete this form by EOD, September 5, to join our teams. Team Sign Up FORM

Important Information, Requirements & Dates:
No paddling/rowing experience needed!  Enjoy a day full of fun, competition, and recreation. Boat teams from all over Long Island and NYC will race. 
Breakfast and lunch will be provided on Race Day. Paddlers will receive an SBU Dragon Boat Race team shirt. 
All Seawolves can also join us for the day to cheer us on and enjoy the events 

  • Team Info Meeting: Friday, September 12 from 1:00 - 2:30 pm - UNITI Cultural Center (SB Union 113) Open to all; Required for undergraduate students
  • Team Practice: Friday, September 12 at 5:00 - 6:00 pm - Port Jefferson Harbor Front Park Marina
  • Race Day: Saturday, September 13 - Arrive at 8:45 am  - PJ Harbor Front Park
    The first race starts at 9:00 am; the Festival ends around 5:00 pm with the Awards & Medals Ceremony
  • Participants must be able to swim (life jackets provided)
  • Participants should have transportation; public transportation is available from SBU to PJ Village

Stony Brook's participation in the Dragon Boat Race is sponsored by the Offices of the President, University and Hospital Community Relations, Diversity, Intercultural, and Community Engagement (DICE), and the Office of the Vice President for Student Affairs.

*For more information about the SB Seawolves Boat: Contact Judy Jaquez at judy.jaquez@stonybrook.edu or 631-632-9912.

*For more information about SB Medicine Boat: Contact Kristin Cuomo at kristin.cuomo@stonybrook.edu or 631-632-6451.

Map/Directions: https://maps.app.goo.gl/ra841pT2U6QjM9iY7

name: The Sociology Exchange
description:
Join us for a casual and informative meet-up with a current Graduate Student, Teaching Assistant, PhD Candidate from the Sociology Department, Hannah Judson!

Whether you're already in the major or just curious, this is your chance to hear what it’s really like to be a grad student, get the inside scoop on courses, and learn what TAs wish students knew. Come with questions or just come to connect—this session is all about helping you succeed from the TA’s point of view. FREE PIZZA!

OCTOBER'S GUEST

Hannah Judson, PhD Candidate in Sociology, Graduate Student, Graduate Teaching Assistant & Sociology of TAYLOR SWIFT course instructor!

Bring a friend and make friendship bracelets after we chat with Hannah!

name: Wolfieland 2025
description:
Join us on September 20th from 4 to 11 PM in the SAC Parking Lots 12 & 13! Wolfieland is the Undergraduate Student Government's annual carnival, hosting up to 6,000+ people! Grab your cowboy boots and pop out for some exhilarating rides, inflatables, games, food, giveaways, prizes, music, dancing, and more! To find out more about what's at Wolfieland, check out our brochure at this link. Additionally, from 6-9 pm every hour on the hour, attendees will be randomly selected to win door prizes sponsored by the Auxiliary Services Association (mini fridge, guitar, bicycle, and iPad Pro), and feature performances from PUSO Modern, KBS, Jubilé Latino, and L'Afrique!

 

This event is free for all undergraduate students and is funded by your Student Activity Fee. Free admission wristbands will also be available to the first 750 graduate students who complete the waiver and pick up a bracelet, courtesy of the Graduate Student Organization.

 

Wristbands are required for entry and are only given to students who complete the waiver. Wristbands are required for mechanical rides and to play games/win prizes! Non-students may roam the event, buy food, etc, but will be unable to participate in mechanical rides or play games/win prizes. 

 

Students with wristbands are eligible to receive a free limited-edition Wolfieland shirt while supplies last at our wristband pick-up stations beginning Wednesday, 9/17 (see below for more details). We encourage everyone to pick up their wristband before Saturday, September 20th, to avoid the extreme rush we anticipate at the ticket office the day of the event. For both early and regular pick-up, please bring your SBU ID Card. See hours below:

 

Wednesday, September 17th

9 am to 5 pm, Union L03-01 (Basement Rehearsal Room)

 

Thursday, September 18th

9 am to 5 pm, Behind the Student Union (Facing Student Health Service Building. The line will form between the Union and East Side Dining.)

 

Friday, September 19th

9 am to 5 pm, Behind the Student Union (Facing Student Health Service Building. The line will form between the Union and East Side Dining.)
7:30 pm to 7:45 pm, Staller Lobby before Superman Viewing

 

Saturday, September 20th

9 am to 4 pm, SAC Ticket Office
4 pm to 11 pm, at Wolfieland at our two registration tents, one closest to the Student Activities Center, and the other, closest to West Side Dining

 

Please note: Wolfieland is a great time, but it's a long time & a highly attended event with large crowds. Prepare accordingly! Bring a charged phone, water, sunscreen, bug spray, & patience as there will be long lines.

 

For any accessibility needs/accommodations, please contact usg_studentlife@stonybrook.edu.

name: VSA Field Day!
description:
Get your energy UP for VSA Field Day! 🌟🎉✨💫 Grab your name tag 🏷️, stretch it out with some friends, and dive headfirst into the FUNNEST night ever! We’re talking wild potato sack races 🥔🏃💨, partner dashes 👯💥, and epic team relays.🏅🥳Plus, some egg-and-spoon battles 🥚🥄🤣 for shiny gold medals 🥇🏆🎖️! Not in a racing mood? No worries, slide on over to the chill zone 🧊🃏🌿, where you’ll find card games 🃏, hilarious icebreakers 🤔🎲, poker headbands 🏷️🤪, and silly games like “Never Have I Ever” 😂🙌 or “Would You Rather” 🧐💬. Meet new friends 🧑🤝🧑, bond with cabinet 💬👋💫, snack on tasty treats 🍪🥤, and hydrate like a champ 💧😋. Good music 🎶, cozy chats 🗣️, happy dances 💃🕺, and all the VSA sparkle you love 💖🌈✨! Bold in the field 💪🔥, laid-back in the chill lounge 🌻🛋️pick your vibe and run with it! Hug it out 🤗, share some laughs 😂, and make the memories of a LIFETIME 📸🌟! Don’t miss out! Run, play, snack, and CHILL your heart out at Field Day! 😊✨🦋🌞

name: Native American Heritage Month Celebration & Panel Discussion
description:
2025 Native American Heritage Month Celebration & PANEL DISCUSSION
Wednesday, November 12, 2025
4:00 PM - 6:00 PM | Zuccaire Gallery, Staller Center

Join us for Stony Brook University’s 2025 Native American Heritage Month Celebration!

The event will feature a spoken word performance by Sumaq Kilari, a viewing of the Zuccaire Gallery exhibition Weaving Words, Weaving Worlds: The Power of Indigenous Language in Contemporary Art, and a Panel Discussion with faculty from Native American and Indigenous Studies (NAIS). 

Panel Discussion Moderator: Joseph Pierce, Director of NAIS and Associate Professor, Department of Hispanic Languages and Literature

Panelists:

  • David Weiden, Department of English
  • Valeria Meiller, Department of Hispanic Languages and Literature
  • Vick Quezada, Department of Art
  • Darcey Evans, Department of Anthropology

Reception with light refreshments to follow. THIS EVENT IS FREE AND OPEN TO ALL.

Presented in collaboration with Native American and Indigenous Studies Initiative, the Office of Diversity, Intercultural and Community Engagement, and the Paul W. Zuccaire Gallery

name: iCafe x iGrad: Building Successful Mentor & Supervisor Relationships

Learn strategies to foster strong, positive, and productive connections with your mentors and supervisors. This workshop will cover effective communication, setting expectations, and navigating challenges to help you get the most out of these professional relationships.

Presented by Dr. Charles Haddad, School of Communication and Journalism.

name: Election Day Celebration
description:
Celebrate Election Day with the Center for Civic Justice, this November 4th!

Join us on Election Day from 8AM to 8PM at the Union Patio for a full day of Election Day programming and information. Representatives from the Center for Civic Justice will be available to provide last-minute assistance to help campus community members successfully cast their ballot.

Additionally, each hour will feature a takeover hosted in collaboration with numerous community partners. Themes range from crafting to stress relief, to food and fun. These events will offer a chance to connect and celebrate the democratic process, and appreciate our ability to be a part of it! The schedule for Election Day is: 

Bagels and Ballots | 8AM - 10AM

Seawolves Pantry Information Station | 10AM-11AM

Pizza at the Polls | 11AM - 1PM 

Fraternity and Sorority Life: "From Greek Row to Capital Hill" | 1PM-2PM

Donuts for Democracy | 2PM-4PM

Destress with the Student Support Team and CPO | 4PM-5PM

Sip and Submit your Ballot with Hot Chocolate | 5PM - 8PM

 

Students who live off-campus can still join us from 8:00AM to 8:00PM for a day full of activities including giveaways, crafts, Uncle Sam, photo ops, "I Voted" stickers and more!

name: Global CommuniTEA: What's in a Meme

name: Sunrise GBM
description:
Sunrise SBU GBMs are a weekly space where student climate activists, organizers, and new members come together to connect, plan, and build collective power to fight for climate justice at Stony Brook University, in New York State, and across the nation.

Sunrise SBU is a hub of the Sunrise Movement, a youth-led movement to stop climate change and create millions of good jobs through a Green New Deal. As a hub, we work to empower students to take action through grassroots organizing, political education, and coalition-building.

description: Blackout X: Afrofluence. An annual  event hosted by BWA and ASU. This years theme is inspired by the African diaspora and Africa's influence on the world. We’re   trying to start the year off right and gather as a community to eat, dance, play games and simply enjoy.

name: SBU Comp Prog Weekly Meeting
description:
Every week, we meet on Tuesday at 7pm in the Old CS Building Room 2114. At each meeting, we hold a practice contest on vjudge.com, and we have free pizza. 

Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club is a place for anyone interested in algorithms and programming. We offer lectures on algorithmic problems solving, host speakers to discuss algorithms, and provide training sessions for our ICPC teams.
The weekly contest takes place on vjudge.com, where participants are presented with a set of problems to solve within specified time and memory constraints. Solutions can be submitted in various programming languages, including C, C++, Java, and Python, among others. All students from SBU are encouraged to participate. We usually have a collaborative environment where everyone can seek assistance from each other. If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!

name: STEM Carnival
description: The 2025 Stony Brook iGEM Team is excited to host a STEM Carnival in collaboration with campus organizations, including NMDP, APSA, ACS, Medlife, BMES, Bioethics, and many more. This event will feature engaging STEM-themed games, interactive activities, and exciting opportunities to connect with student clubs that you can get involved with on campus. You’ll also learn about research opportunities, outreach initiatives, and ways to build community in science and medicine at Stony Brook. Plus, attendees will have the chance to win amazing prizes such as a heated blanket, Japanese snack and candy box, book nook kit, matcha kit, ice maker machine, Egg Jellycat, electric ramen cooker, etc. Don’t miss this fun and rewarding way to explore STEM while discovering new ways to get involved!

name: Machine Learning Workshop: GBM #1
description: Curious about AI but not sure where to start? This workshop walks you through the core ideas of Machine Learning—how models learn from data, how to train them, and how to evaluate performance. You’ll build simple ML models from scratch and understand how they’re used in real-world applications. No prior ML experience needed—just a willingness to learn.

name: Community @UNITI
description:
Join USG and DICE in the UNITI Cultural Center for the 4th annual Community @UNITI 🎤☕️

Come through for a cozy night filled with poetry, music, and stories at our open mic! We’ll have coffee, hot cocoa, donuts, and munchkins waiting for you 🍩☕️

 This year’s lineup includes live performances from At-Large Senator Carly Dunn and Omar Khan, performances you won’t want to miss!

Whether you’re stepping up to the mic or just hanging out, Community @UNITI is all about connection, creativity, and good vibes. It’s always a campus favorite, and this semester’s first event is hosted by VP of University Affairs Ece Naz Durali.

See you Wednesday night at UNITI!

name: Wellness Fair
description:
Join the Skincare and Wellness Club for our third annual Wellness Fair! This year's theme is Beauty and the BS, and with it, the Wellness Fair will dive into common myths about wellness, highlight sustainable and accessible self-care practices, and empower attendees to make informed choices for their health and the planet.

By working with 13 collaborating clubs and organizations, we're bringing you a campus-wide celebration of health & community. Here's what you can expect to find at the Wellness Fair:

Plenty of free swag, including raffle prizes

DIY Skincare and other creative crafts

Snacks & refreshments

Wellness tips and resources

And a newfound sense of clarity and confidence in your self-care routines!

 

Whether you’re looking for stress relief, sustainable skincare ideas, or simply a supportive community, there’s something here for everyone.

 

We hope to see you there! Please reach out to sbu.skincare@gmail.com with any accessibility concerns.

name: CPA Bid day Celebration
description: Celebrate Panhellenic Bid Day with all three Panhellenic sororities at the Shore Club! This event will welcome new members into our Greek community with a celebration featuring mocktails and an opportunity to connect with sisters from across chapters. Along with the celebration, there will be a short informational session to introduce new members to Panhellenic life, traditions, and upcoming opportunities.

Meet up with fellow Seawolves with children for some fall-themed fun, including crafts, family bonding, brunch refreshments, and Stony Brook swag raffles! 

With Halloween just a few weeks away -- COSTUMES ARE WELCOME!! Children will also be able to customize 'trick or treat' goodie bags. 

 

RSVP to let us know you're planning to attend!

Reach out to student_community_development@stonybrook.edu with any questions!

 

At this time this event is only open to Stony Brook Students who have children (parents/guardians/caregiver). Minors must be accompanied by adults at ALL times. Stony Brook University is NOT responsible for care, custody, or supervision of minors.

name: A Selenabration of Hispanic Heritage Month! Selena Movie & Craft Night
description:
A Selenabration of Hispanic Heritage Month
Saturday, October 18, 2025 | 7:00 PM – 10:00 PM
Student Activities Center, Ballroom B

Join us for a vibrant evening of music, nostalgia, and creativity as we honor Hispanic Heritage Month with A Selenabration dedicated to the Queen of Tejano, Selena Quintanilla-Pérez. Inspired by the beloved Selena movie, the night will feature themed crafts, throwback vibes, and an atmosphere filled with cultura and community.

Come dressed in your best Selena-inspired look, enjoy make & take crafting activities, and sing along to the classics that continue to inspire generations. Whether you’re reliving the magic of the movie or discovering it for the first time, this night promises joy, connection, and celebration.

This event is free and open to all Stony Brook students, faculty, and staff.

Closet Remix offers students the opportunity to refresh their wardrobes while learning how to resell clothing through platforms such as Depop. ✨The event will include a workshop on creating a successful online shop— from photographing and listing items to pricing and marketing strategies — as well as a clothing swap where participants can bring gently used pieces to exchange for new-to-you finds.✨

Whether you are looking to explore resale platforms, give your clothing a second life, or discover unique styles from your peers, this event provides a space to learn, connect, and elevate your personal style responsibly. 🌱

name: E&E Colloquium: "The Building Blocks of Vertebrate Diversity" by Dr. Laura Alencar
description:
Vertebrates range from ray-finned fish to salamanders to humans—so what drives this incredible diversity?

Dr. Laura Alencar seeks to answer this very question in her talk, "The Building Blocks of Vertebrate Diversity." She will explore the key innovations that make this species so diverse and how these factors accelerates or constrain evolution. If you are interested in learning more about vertebrates then you won't want to miss this.

Date: Wednesday, November 5, 2025 | 2:15 PM
Location: Life Sciences Building, Room 038

Don’t miss it if you're curious about what makes vertebrates so unique!

name: Women in Science Panel
description:
Ever wonder what it’s like to build a career in science? Curious about the paths researchers and professors take and the challenges and breakthroughs along the way?

Join WiPA for our Women in Science Panel, featuring inspiring researchers and professors across various fields of STEM. They’ll share their journeys, insights on research, and advice for students interested in science and academia.

This is your chance to hear real stories, ask questions, and connect with role models making an impact in their fields.

Refreshments will be provided, so come for the conversation and stay for the snacks!

name: GBM #4: S&P Global - Machine Learning in Finance
description:
💡Curious how Artificial Intelligence is transforming the world of finance? Join us for lunch 🥪 and an engaging panel discussion with industry experts from S&P Global 📊, where we’ll dive deep into how AI and data-driven models are transforming everything from market analysis to risk management 💰🤖

🎙️Our panelists will share their insights on:

  • What it’s really like to apply machine learning in finance, from predictive modeling to natural language processing 🖥️
  • The skills and mindsets that helped them succeed in this rapidly evolving field 
  • The real-world challenges of working with complex, ever-changing financial data 📋
  • How to build a career at the intersection of AI and finance, and what students can do right now to get started 💼

✨ Whether you’re passionate about finance, data science, or AI, this event is your chance to gain first-hand knowledge and advice from leaders at the forefront of financial innovation 🌍📈

name: Finals Fuel-Up
description:
Take a break from the end-of-semester chaos and recharge with your community! Join us for a cozy board game night where you can unwind, de-stress, and enjoy some friendly competition. While you’re here, grab a Finals Study Survival Kit—created in partnership with New Student & Transition Programs—packed with snacks, tea, study tools, and other essentials to help you power through finals.
This event is especially helpful for first-year students who are experiencing their first finals week. It’s a chance to connect with peers, learn healthy study habits, ask questions, and pick up resources that make the transition into college academics feel smoother and more manageable.
Come relax, refuel, and get ready to finish the semester strong!

name: GradConnect Series
description:
We are excited to invite graduate students to the November GradConnect Series, organized by the Graduate School.

 

This new monthly series will partner with on-campus and off-campus resources to provide students with direct opportunities to learn about available services and connect. The event will also be a platform for graduate students to interact with each other, share experiences, and build a supportive community.

 

For the  November GradConnect Series,  event, we are delighted that the following campus departments and resources are joining to support and connect with graduate students.

  • Center for Prevention and Outreach (CPO)
  • Counseling and Psychological Services (CAPS) 

 

Light refreshments will be provided.

 

 Plus, attend at least three of the four Fall 2025 events in the GradConnect Series to be entered into our Grand Raffle Prize drawing in December!  

 

Looking forward to seeing you at the GradConnect Series event.

name: Serverless & Supabase:Hackathon Workshop
description:
Want to build full-stack apps with no backend code?

Join the Web Development Club for our hands-on workshop: Build a Serverless App with Supabase

In this workshop, you'll learn how to:

  • Set up a Supabase project with authentication, database, and APIs

  • Build a complete web app using only frontend code

  • Add real-time functionality using Supabase's built-in tools

  • Deploy and share your project quickly — perfect for hackathons

Tech Stack:

  • Database & Auth: Supabase (open-source Firebase alternative)

  • Frontend: React, Vite, Tailwind CSS

  • Dev Tools: Supabase Studio, GitHub Codespaces (no local setup needed)

Spin up a browser-based environment and follow along as we build a serverless app from scratch in under an hour.

If you're interested in fast, modern web development without the backend complexity — this workshop is for you.

Come code with us!

name: Cocoa & Cramming
description: Take a moment to slow down and breathe before finals season hits. Cocoa & Cramming is a cozy wellness space designed to help you unwind and reset. We’ll have simple mindfulness activities—including guided breathing cards, reflection prompts, and a small gratitude board—to help you release stress and feel grounded. Whether you stay for five minutes or the whole time, this night is all about taking care of your mind and giving yourself a well-deserved break.

name: iGrad: Intercultural Communication and Professional Etiquette
description:
This session is designed to equip international graduate students with the essential interpersonal and professional skills necessary for success in a globalized workplace. The workshop will cover key aspects of professional etiquette in a Western context, including proper handshaking, exchanging business cards, and the art of networking. Participants will learn how to craft an effective elevator pitch, communicate their professional goals clearly and concisely, and build confidence in professional settings. The goal is to bridge cultural gaps and provide students with the tools to navigate professional environments with poise and competence.

Presented by Jessica Feliz, Director of International Graduate Programs for the College of Business.

name: National Voter Registration Day
description:
We’re excited to once again be part of the national effort to strengthen our democracy by registering voters on this year’s National Voter Registration Day, which will take place on September 17th, 2025!
 
First celebrated in 2012, National Voter Registration Day is a nonpartisan holiday that’s helped more than 5 million people register to vote and ensure they are ready to hit the polls in their next election. 
 
This year's National Voter Registration Day will take place on Wednesday, September 18th from 12:00PM- 2:00PM in the SAC Plaza!
 
Engage with our many student organizations and departmental partners, grab some prizes and giveaways, and learn more about how you can be prepared to participate in the upcoming election.

name: Community Dialogue: LGBTQ Rights and Local Government
description:
Join us for an open and welcoming conversation exploring the intersections between LGBTQ+ identity and local government. This dialogue is meant to empower, encourage, and mobilize LGBTQ+ students and allies by creating a space to share stories, ask questions, and build community.

Together, we’ll talk about the fears and challenges LGBTQ+ people may be facing today, and how those challenges can be met with strength, solidarity, and civic engagement. We’ll also highlight the power of local government: the policies and decisions that directly shape our daily lives, the opportunities for involvement, and the importance of lifting LGBTQ+ voices in our own neighborhoods.

name: SBU Comp Prog Weekly Meeting
description:
Every week, we meet on Tuesday at 7pm in the Old CS Building Room 2114. At each meeting, we hold a practice contest on vjudge.com, and we have free pizza. 

Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club is a place for anyone interested in algorithms and programming. We offer lectures on algorithmic problems solving, host speakers to discuss algorithms, and provide training sessions for our ICPC teams.
The weekly contest takes place on vjudge.com, where participants are presented with a set of problems to solve within specified time and memory constraints. Solutions can be submitted in various programming languages, including C, C++, Java, and Python, among others. All students from SBU are encouraged to participate. We usually have a collaborative environment where everyone can seek assistance from each other. If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!

name: NRHH's Sustainability Week
description:
Get ready for a week full of green vibes, creative reuse, and community impact! NRHH is celebrating Sustainability Week with a lineup of events that bring environmental action to life — from peaceful nature walks and hands-on campus cleanups to DIY projects that turn trash into treasure. Whether you're folding boats from recycled paper, digging into a little gardening, or just taking in the fresh air by Roth Pond, there's something for everyone! 

Lasting Monday 9/29 to Friday 10/3.

 

Monday, 9/29 – Nature Walk

Time: 4:00 PM – 5:00 PM

Meet outside Javits by the bike station. We'll walk around Javits, then head toward Roth Pond for a walk around the water. The walk will end either back at the bike station or near the pond.

 

Tuesday, 9/30 – Campus Clean-Up

Time: 1:30 PM – 3:00 PM

Follow the same route as Monday’s walk, but this time we’ll focus on picking up trash along the way. Bring gloves if you have them!

 

Wednesday, 10/1 – Plastic Bottle DIY & Gardening

Time: 5:00 PM – 6:00 PM

Join us outside Mendy at the red tables between Gray and Ammann for a fun DIY session using plastic bottles to create simple gardening containers.

 

Thursday, 10/2 – Origami Night: Folding with Recycled Paper

Time: 7:00 PM - 8:00 PM

Come join us for a relaxing origami night where we’ll be folding paper boats and other designs using recycled paper. As part of our sustainability efforts, this event highlights the importance of reusing paper in creative ways. No experience needed — just drop in, fold, and have fun while being eco-conscious!

 

Friday, 10/3 – Nature Walk: Part 2

Time: 4:00 PM – 5:00 PM

If you missed the first one — or just want another chance to unwind outdoors — join us for Nature Walk: Part 2. We'll meet outside Javits by the bike station, walk through the same route around Javits, and head back toward Roth Pond. Along the way, we’ll take time to enjoy the scenery, chat, and reflect on the week. Feel free to bring a friend, a reusable water bottle, or just yourself!

address.name: SAC 304, Roth, Javits Center, and Between Ammann and Gray in Mendelsohn.

The goal is to create a fun and engaging space where students can share the supernatural creature stories and folktales many of us grew up hearing, along with personal experiences tied to them. It will be both entertaining and cultural, giving students the chance to connect, learn from each other, and build community in a relaxed setting.

This event will encourage interaction, allow students to celebrate Halloween with a cultural twist, and provide a unique way for people to meet and engage with each other.

Whether you're exploring sociology or already part of the department, all are welcome!

November Feature: Dr. Nicholas Bascuñan-Wiley, Assistant Professor
Dr. Bascuñan-Wiley's research interests include:

Migration; Culture; Globalization; Foodways; Sensation/The Body; Ethnography

Free Pizza & Giveaways!

11/16/2025 at 8:15PM @ West E Commons and create whatever your imagination thinks of. Feel free to bring whatever you need! Try your best to make your work stand out and tell its own unique story!

We will be evaluating art based on a few categories: Most thought-provoking (make people wonder about your creation!) - How unique the art is (get creative and make your work stand out from the others!) - Story telling (a good story always gives a new interesting perspective. Make your art tell your own unique story!) 

There will be chips and drinks available if you stop by! 

 

Hope to see you there!

Any questions? Send an email to: lennie.gleyzer@stonybrook.edu or edward.cibu@stonybrook.edu

This event is located in the Tabler Arts Center Blackbox Theater on October 25th from 3pm-8pm! There will be shirts and merchandise available by donations, and complimentary refreshments!

name: Rohan Pradhan Guest Speech by Amazon

Students will have the opportunity to hear from Rohan Pradhan, Applied Scientist at Amazon and a leading voice in Generative AI and Frontier Models research.

The event will feature:

• 🎤 Talk: A presentation by Rohan Pradhan on the reliability of AI systems

• ❓ Q&A: An open forum for student questions

name: Point72/Cubist Systematic Strategies Information Panel
description:
Register on Handshake!

 

Point72/ Cubist Systematic Strategies will be hosting a Lunch and Learn on Wednesday, October 29h at (SAC 302). Sign up to meet with researchers and recruiters, learn more about internship and full-time opportunities at Cubist, pick up the latest swag, and of course enjoy lunch!

Cubist designs and implements systematic, computer-driven trading strategies based on rigorous research to invest across multiple liquid asset classes. Our teams around the world develop their investment theses from our extensive access to a wide range of publicly available data. Our colleagues come from other firms, related fields, and directly from academia. Whatever your background, here we all share a passion for problem solving, strong technical skills, and a commitment to the highest professional and ethical standards.

description:

Join us and the first 300 participants will get a voucher for food and refreshment for the event.

This is your chance to:

Free Catering Provided!

Whether you’re a seasoned journalist or just newspaper-curious, everyone is welcome.

Bring your appetite and your ideas — see you there!

name: SBU Comp Prog Weekly Meeting
description:
Every week, we meet on Tuesday at 7pm in the Old CS Building Room 2114. At each meeting, we hold a practice contest on vjudge.com, and we have free pizza. 

Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club is a place for anyone interested in algorithms and programming. We offer lectures on algorithmic problems solving, host speakers to discuss algorithms, and provide training sessions for our ICPC teams.
The weekly contest takes place on vjudge.com, where participants are presented with a set of problems to solve within specified time and memory constraints. Solutions can be submitted in various programming languages, including C, C++, Java, and Python, among others. All students from SBU are encouraged to participate. We usually have a collaborative environment where everyone can seek assistance from each other. If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!

name: SBU Comp Prog Weekly Meeting
description:

If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!

name: A Cold War Era Murder Mystery
description:
You've been invited to attend a diplomatic meeting. Tensions are high with regional conflicts already putting millions of lives at stake, and the thought of mutually-assured destruction is looming over your heads. However, things take a sharp turn when one of the diplomats is murdered. Can your table find out who murdered them and why before hysteria takes over? Trust nobody, not even yourselves. 

In this Cold War murder mystery, it is on a first-come, first-served basis. There will be free food and drinks. The team that figures out the motive and who killed our diplomat will win a prize!

name: Tech & Engineering Internship Application Workshop
description:
In this event, students will learn tips and tricks for applying to tech and engineering internships for this upcoming summer. Students will learn about resume writing techniques, networking, ways to find opportunities, interviewing tips, and on-campus resources available to them, such as the Career Center! This event will focus exclusively on the competitive application process for tech and engineering internships. Internship search strategies will also be shared. Light refreshments will be provided!

About the organizers:

RA Samuel Perrottet is a senior in the Computer Science major. He has completed one summer internship at Betterment, a wealth management firm, where he will continue working after graduation this spring. He has also worked on campus as an assistant at the SeaWulf supercomputer cluster.

RA Joseph Litjens is a senior in the Mechanical Engineering major. He has completed a summer internship at ASML, a semiconductor manufacturing equipment company, as well as an internship at Brookhaven National Laboratory, a national scientific research hub just a short drive away from campus.

For comments, questions, or concerns, please reach out to joseph.litjens@stonybrook.edu or samuel.perrottet@stonybrook.edu

We're hosting special guest Ariek Norford who will present: “Monkeys: The Gardeners of the Forest” - Get a wild look at how both common and threatened monkey species play a key role in seed dispersal and forest health. We’ll also be talking about career paths in the Ecology & Evolution field and how you can start getting involved as a student.
 
Free Food, snacks, drinks, and chill vibes guaranteed.
Bring a friend and your curiosity — we’d love to see you there!

This event is free and open to all. Register Here

Hispanic Heritage Month Committee, Universitarios Unidos Latinoamericano (Latin American Faculty & Staff Resource Group), Stony Brook University Alumni Association, Office of Diversity, Inclusion, Intercultural Initiatives (DI3), and Diversity, Intercultural and Community Engagement (DICE)

name: Take to: Keith Haring Coming Out Paint Day
description:
Join RA Maanav as we head over to the Keith Haring Coming Out Paint Day on Tuesday, October 8th! This event celebrates National Coming Out Day through art, reflection, and community.

You will have the chance to:
🎨 Paint Keith Haring–inspired designs on tote bags and canvases
✍️ Write a coming out letter to themselves or others
🖌️ Color in iconic artwork inspired by Keith Haring

This is a great opportunity to honor the legacy of Keith Haring as a queer artist and activist, celebrate the courage of LGBTQ+ individuals who live openly, and create space for allies to learn and show support.

Come join us for art, community, and conversation—you don’t need any art experience, just bring yourself!

We will meet at the Chapin MPR at 11:30 AM and take the bus to SAC!

name: Toy Adaptation
description: Toy Adaptation Event is a hands-on workshop where students will team up with industry professionals to modify commercially available toys to make them accessible for children with physical and cognitive disabilities. Participants will learn the basics of circuit wiring, soldering, and toy mechanics in a collaborative and educational setting. Adapted toys will be donated to families and local therapy centers/schools. This event is aimed at raising awareness around assistive technology and inclusive design. No prior experience is required — we will provide all the materials, tools, and guidance needed.

name: Ecology and Evolutions Society Club: From Mouths to Mangroves (Microbial Ecology)
description: Ever wonder how tiny microbes shape the world around us? Come to our event, "Microbial Ecology: From Mouths to Mangroves" to explore how microorganisms live everywhere—from our own mouths to muddy mangrove forests! Our speaker will share cool stories from their research on the human oral microbiome and a microbe that helps break down detritus in mangrove soil. They’ll also talk a bit about their path in science and taking time between undergrad and grad school. Come learn, ask questions, and see why microbial ecology is way more exciting than you might think!

name: Podcast Boot Camp - Prepare for the NPR Podcast Challenge to win a $5000 Prize and Compete to win Campus Prizes.
description:
RSVP Via QR Code or here on SB engaged Required. Sign up for one session only Breakfast and Giveaways. 1st,2nd and 3rd prizes.

Friday Nov 21st or Friday December 5th 10:00-12:30pm SAC 307

Join The Society of Professional Journalists, The Student Media Council and WUSB radio station for a podcast bootcamp to prepare you to submit for the National Public Radio podcast challenge in 2026. You have a chance to win a $5000 NPR prize plus prizes and give aways from Student Community Development's Student Media Service Office.

name: Cultural Immersion
description:
Join ✈️Paper Airplanes✈️ to learn🤓 about the diverse cultures of our students👩🏽‍🏫.

 

Paper Airplanes✈️ tutors refugees all over the world🌍, namely Syria🇸🇾 and Ukraine🇺🇦. We will be playing a trivia game and celebrating our student cultures with tea🫖, stamps💟, and baklava.
