name: TC Presents: Air Dry Clay Making
description:
Looking to get creative without the mess of paint and glue? 🎨🎨🎨Guess what — Tzu Ching is hosting Air-Dry Clay Making! ✈️💨💨💨Join us for a relaxing 🌿🌿😴😴and hands-on evening 🫷🏻where you can mold, shape, and design your own clay masterpiece — all with easy-to-use air-dry clay. 🛍️🛍️🛍️No chaos, no cleanup, just pure creativity! 🆓🆓

Free drinks and snacks on arrival! 🤤🤤🤤

Tabler 107

Nov 07, 2025: 6:30-7:30PM

20 Cap

name: November Blood Drive
description:
The SBU Student Blood Drive Committee works with the New York Blood Center to facilitate the donation of blood that will save lives across the state of New York. The NYBC provides the medical equipment and personnel, and the SBDC helps set up the space at Stony Brook and provides volunteers! 

ALL donors will receive a free Beanie from the New York Blood Center and a $5 USG Voucher courtesy of USG (while supplies last).

name: Surprise Movie Screening With CSA
description:
🎬 Join CSA for a surprise movie screening! You'll have to come to the GBM to find out 👀🍿 

Take a break in between classes and enjoy a fun mid-day movie with the Commuter Student Association! We’ll be showing a popular film (TBA at the event 👀), and we’ve got FREE popcorn and candy for everyone who attends.

Whether you’re commuting to campus or hanging around between lectures, swing by, relax, and spend some time with friends!

📍 Student Activities Center 305
🕒 12:30 PM – 1:50 PM

This event is open to all students, bring your friends and come have a great time with us!

name: Pilates X Women in Business Collab
description: Please join us for our collaboration event with the Women in Business Club! We will be hosting a guided pilates workout along with homemade brownies. Whether you're a beginner or experienced, come to stretch, strengthen, and connect with fellow fitness enthusiasts. We’ll guide you through a series of exercises that focus on core strength, posture, and body awareness. Bring your mat (if you have one), a water bottle, and a positive attitude – we can’t wait to move with you!🧘‍♀️ 💗

name: PPGA's Movie Night: Heightened Scrutiny
description:
Join us to watch the film Heightened Scrutiny! 

"Fearless civil rights lawyer Chase Strangio battles at the Supreme Court for transgender adolescents’ access to gender-affirming healthcare, confronting not only the legal system but also a media landscape that distorts public perception and threatens the struggle for trans rights. With the dangerous SCOTUS decision upholding the ban on life-saving healthcare, HEIGHTENED SCRUTINY is an urgent call to action against bigotry and injustice."

-https://www.heightenedscrutinydoc.com/

name: Mind Body Workshop
description: Join us in a holistic event combining mindfulness practices, light movement such as stretching and yoga, and discuss about the importance of balancing mental and physical health. There will be protein cupcakes provided at the end!

name: GBM 4: Donation Kits & 2026 E-Board Reveal
description:
Join the Pre-Nursing Society for our final event of the semester as we give back to the community and celebrate together! 

We’ll be assembling hygiene donation kits for ECLI–VIBES, a local domestic violence shelter that provides essential support to individuals and families in need. This hands-on service activity is a great way to make a meaningful impact while connecting with fellow members.

After the donation kits are completed, we’ll reveal the 2026 Executive Board! Come show your support, meet the new leadership team, and celebrate another amazing semester together.

Pizza will be served for all attendees! 

This event is worth 1 point for all Pre-Nursing Society members!

You MUST RSVP to reserve your spot at this popular event!

name: Overnight Oats Event
description: Join us in making a healthy breakfast option: Overnight Oats!

name: Thankful Bingo Bash
description: B-I-N-G-O and cocoa makes the perfect combo! Join PALs Emily, Jerry, Ryan, Taaha, Amanda, and Kelly to celebrate Thanksgiving the fun way. Play a round of Bingo, paired with delicious cookies and steaming mugs of hot chocolate. Win prizes, make memories, and leave feeling just as warm and happy as a fresh pumpkin pie! 🧡

name: NRHH GBM #6: Movie Night!
description: The semester’s wrapping up, so take a break and relax with NRHH Thursday, November 13th at 7 PM in SAC 304! Come kick back, enjoy a movie, and hang out with friends before finals season hits. We’ll have snacks, chill vibes, and a great way to end the week. Don’t miss it!

name: Panda Brawlers
description:
Get ready to laugh, play, and compete in the most panda-monium event of the semester! 🐾

Panda Brawlers is all about bringing people together for a day of fun mini games, prizes, and good vibes 💥 Come join us as different clubs table and take part in the friendly chaos — you might even walk away with a prize (or a new friend)!

🎯 What’s Happening:
• Play interactive mini games and win awesome prizes + trinkets 🏅
• Hang out with different campus clubs as they join in the fun 🤝
• Enjoy FREE Taichi popcorn chicken 🍗 and Sugar Miss boba because games are always better with snacks!

Don’t miss your chance to brawl it out (the fun way) and make some unforgettable memories 🐼✨

name: SSA Shukrana Kirtan & Simran
description: The Sikh student Association is holding a prayer off campus in thanks for the semester and in celebration of Thanksgiving.

address.name: Gurudwara Guru Gobind Singh Sikh Center

name: Hot Cocoa GBM
description: Brrr, it's getting cold outside! Join Wagner Hall in a cozy night of hot chocolate and other delights. You can also listen to our Vice President talk about future Wagner Hall things!

name: Pizza and Policies
description: Come join RA Tolani to learn more about our local government roles (mayor, reps, council), how voting works, and where to find polling locations. Hosted on Saturday, December 6th, 2026, at 2pm in Whitman Hall Lounge.

address.address: 700 Health Science, Stony Brook, New York
address.name: Whitman Hall

name: Hanukkah Arts and Crafts Night
description: Spin into the last event of the semester with us! Join Chabad for a cozy Hanukkah arts & crafts night featuring fill-your-own donut holes (aka DIY mini sufganiyot!). Come unwind, get creative, and end the semester on a sweet note!

name: Friendsgiving at Chapin Apartments
description:
Chapin Apartments presents Friendsgiving! Join us on Wednesday, November 26 at 5:00 PM in the Chapin MPR for an evening of great food, laughter, and community. Take a break before the holiday season to enjoy a cozy meal with fellow residents and celebrate friendship, gratitude, and togetherness.

Food and drinks will be provided, so come hungry and bring your Chapin spirit! Whether you’re far from home or just looking to share good vibes, this is the perfect opportunity to connect, relax, and make memories with friends old and new.

Capped at 40. Join the fun while supplies last!

address.address: 700 Health Science Dr Commons, Stony Brook , 11790
address.name: Chapin Commons

name: Safe Sex Jeapordy x The Condom Collective
description:
Join RA Rubbab and RA Jessica for a fun, judgment-free night of Safe Sex Jeopardy, hosted in partnership with Advocates for Youth’s Condom Collective Campaign! Come test your knowledge on sexual health, consent, protection, and everything in between while learning practical tips for safer sex. This interactive game night is designed to empower students with accurate information, break stigmas, and promote confident, healthy decision-making.

📅 Date: December 4, 2025
📍 Location: West E MPR
⏰ Time: 7:00 PM – 8:00 PM

Bring your competitive spirit—winners will receive extra entries into our raffle, including a chance to win a sex toy! Free safer-sex supplies from the Condom Collective will also be available for all attendees.

Lauterbur Residents eligible to recive Civic Engagement Credit.

If you have any accesibility concerns please contact Rubbab.SyedJaffary@stonybrook.edu or Jessica.Sandoval@stonybrook.edu.

name: E&E Seminar: Disease dynamics under global change: amphibian declines and resilience in a multi-stressor world
description:
Join us for our Ecology & Evolution Seminar on Tuesday, December 9th, 2025 at 10: 00 AM in Liaufer Center!

We’re excited to welcome Dr. Michel Ohmer, Ecophysiologist at the University of Mississippi, as she presents:
"Disease dynamics under global change: amphibian declines and resilience in a multi-stressor world."

name: Bengalis Unite Fourth GBM
description: Join Bengalis Unite for our last event of the semester, Movie Night! We will be enjoying some yummy jhal muri, drinking warm cha, and watching Bangla short films. A perfect way to destress before finals and get prepared for winter break!

name: Noche de Baile
description:
Get ready for a night full of music, culture, and movement! Noche de Baile is a celebration of Latin rhythm and community, brought to you by Jubilé Latino. Join us as we fill the floor with the vibrant sounds of salsa, bachata, merengue, reggaetón, and more!

Whether you’re a beginner learning your first steps or a seasoned dancer ready to show off your style, this event is all about joy, connection, and embracing Latinx culture through dance. Come with friends, meet new people, and enjoy an unforgettable night in a space filled with energy and positivity.

Let’s light up the night together. ¡Nos vemos en la pista! ✨

name: GSO END-OF-SEMESTER PARTY - 2025
description:
This semester’s been a grind for everyone, and this is that one evening where you just show up, unwind, and enjoy the vibe. The GSO End-of-Semester Party on 3rd December 2025 is going to have everything you’d hope for - good food, live performances, music, and a full DJ set to close out the night.

We’re keeping the atmosphere fun and relaxed. If you want to come, eat, chill with friends, take photos, dance a little, or just sit back and enjoy the performances, you’re welcome. If you want to perform, even better - we love seeing students bring their talent on stage.

We’ll be putting together a short lineup of performers, so we’re opening an Interest Form for anyone who wants a slot. Here’s what you should know before signing up:

  • Filling the form does not guarantee a performance slot.

  • Slots will be decided on a first-come, first-serve basis.

  • The final lineup depends on the available time and flow of the event.

  • If you're selected, we’ll reach out with confirmation and details.

If you’re interested in performing (music, dance, stand-up, poetry - anything you’d like to showcase), fill out the form here:

👉 Performance Interest Form: https://forms.gle/fdCNjcKP5Zvn9B3N8

 

Come for the food. Come for the music. Come for the vibe.


See you on December 3rd at SAC Ballroom A, 6 PM to 8 PM.

address.address: 100 Nicolls Rd, Stony Brook University, NY
address.name: SAC Ballroom A

name: Seasons of Sustainability
description:
Join RA Isabella, RA Gulika, and PAL Saifa for Seasons of Sustainability, a cozy, eco-friendly holiday crafting event! Celebrate the start of December by creating your own winter ornaments using recycled and natural materials. Learn simple ways to incorporate sustainability into seasonal traditions while enjoying a relaxing, creative evening with your community.

All attendees will be entered for a chance to WIN essential dorm necessities—perfect for keeping your room warm and cozy through the colder months!

This event is hosted in collaboration with the New Student and Transition Program (NSTP).

📍 Location: Uniti Cultural Center, Stony Brook Union
📅 Date & Time: Monday, December 1st, 7:30–9:00 PM
♻️ Activity: DIY eco-friendly holiday ornaments
🎁 Special: Raffle for dorm necessities

address.name: Uniti Cultural Center at the Stony Brook Union

name: Hot Cocoa Recap - End of Semester Floor Meeting
description: On December 1 at 7:00pm, join RA Ysabel and RA Scarlett for our end of semester floor meeting. We'll be having free hot chocolate for everyone as well ^_^

name: Holiday Paint Night
description: In this event, residents will paint holiday pictures to destress before finals! They meet new people and learn about other holiday traditions. Holiday themed cookies and candy will be provided, as well as canvases for artwork!

name: Open Mic Night
description: The Press Open Mic Night is back! We are hosting an open mic night to allow current members and newcomers to showcase their talents! We will be having a small lineup of preplanned performances, and then open the floor up to anyone who has music, poetry, short stories, or anything else they might want to share. If you plan on performing, please fill out this form.

name: USG Presents: Study Kits!
description:
Finals coming up? We’ve got you covered! ✏️✨

Build your own USG Study Kit-packed with study essentials, snacks, self-care items, and fun stress-relief goodies to help you power through the semester. Stop by, pick your favorites, and get ready to finish strong! 💛

📍 Union Lobby:
• Wed, Dec 3 | 12:30–1:50 PM

📍 SAC Lobby:
• Fri, Dec 5 | 4:00–6:00 PM
• Mon, Dec 8 | 12:30–1:50 PM

Come early while supplies last! Free for all students.

name: Studyathon
description:
📚 Ready to crush your study session on Reading Day? Join RAs Maxime, Justin, Cibu, and Jouzef at The Studyathon for a power-packed study event that’ll keep you fueled and focused!

📅 When: Tuesday, December 9th, 12:30 PM - 2:30 PM
📍 Where: West E Commons, MPR
🍔 What's on the Menu: Buffalo Wild Wings + Celsius Energy Drinks (because nothing fuels a study grind like wings and energy drinks!)

Lock in with us: whether you’re tackling assignments, reviewing for finals, or just need a quiet place to study. Come for the food, stay for the focus!

Feel free to stick around after 2:30 PM if you want to keep studying, this is your space to stay as long as you need!

✨ Why Attend?

  • Free Buffalo Wild Wings and Celsius Energy Drinks to keep you energized!

  • A relaxed, study-friendly space to help you prep for finals week.

  • Perfect spot to meet up with friends or make some new ones while studying!

Come grab some wings, sip on a Celsius, and get ready to ace your finals!

name: 2YLC Health and Wellness: Sweet Study Break
description:
Join RA Erik for a Sweet Study Break, where you can decorate delicious cookies and take a breather from classes. Customize your treats with fun designs while you enjoy a cozy, low-pressure space to relax. 2YLC students can earn their Health and Wellness credit. 

 

For Accessibility concerns, email erik.pizzirusso@stonybrook.edu.

name: Tau Beta Pi Bent Decorating
description: Join Tau Beta Pi members in decorating the bent located in the lobby of the Engineering Building! Counts for member event credit.

name: Snacks for Finals
description: SBU Libraries will be handing out snacks while you review for finals!

address.name: HSL, Melville Library (North & Central Reading Rooms)

description: Join RA Christian for a cozy, festive break from finals at our Christmas Watch Party! We’ll be watching Jack Frost and enjoying snacks, drinks, and relaxing holiday vibes. Come unwind, hang out with your hallmates, and get into the spirit of the season. PJs and comfy clothes encouraged!

name: New Transfer Student Welcome
description:
Academic & Transfer Advising Services (ATAS) invites you to the New Transfer Student Welcome!

Join us to:

  • Connect with ATAS Advisors
  • Learn more about your advising unit
  • Hear first hand experiences from fellow transfer students
  • Connect with campus resources
  • Get giveaways, raffles and prizes!

FREE lunch will be served!

description:
🔥 Welcome Back HSC BBQ! 🔥
Kick off the semester with free food, awesome giveaways, live music, and fun games! Open to all Health Sciences Center students, faculty, and staff. Don’t miss this chance to connect, celebrate, and win prizes—RSVP today and bring your friends!

name: Intro to VSA
description:
Ready to meet new friends🌟🎉 and kickstart 🏁 your ✨semester✨ with VSA✌️? Come join us 🥳🌸 at "Intro to VSA"🐘🌷 for a 💫night🌙 of getting to know 👀🤝 our 🫶amazing members 👫, meeting new🫂💡 faces 😊, and feasting 🍽️ on fresh 🌱pandan waffles🧇, and refreshing🤩 drinks🍵🧸! Get 🤩 hype for a fun-filled 😆 bingo 🎲 icebreaker, clay 🎨sculpting🎨 games 🤪, and all 🌟 the 💌warm🥰 VSA energy ⚡ you 🫵 could wish 🌠 for! Whether you're new 🆕💗 or 💃 returning 💯, we 🙌 can’t wait 🕒 to see 👓 you🫵 there😚!
Date :Aug 26 🗓️
Time :7–9PM ⏰
Location : SAC Ballroom A 📍
Cap :72

name: Paint N'Dip/Sip & Movie Night
description: Celebrate Labor Day Weekend with Paint N’Dip/Sip Movie Night! Come out to the West E Commons MPR for a night of creativity, snacks, and a movie all in one! Relax with the Schomburg West team as you paint, sip, and snack on chips and dip—all while enjoying a movie in the background. Supplies are provided, so just bring yourself and your friends for a cozy evening of art, laughter, and fun.

name: CKI's First GBM: Movie Night!
description:
Kick off the semester with Circle K International at our first General Body Meeting—and what better way to start than with a Movie Night! 🎥

Join us for a fun and relaxing evening as we screen a fan-favorite film and get to know each other. Whether you're a returning member or new to CKI, this is the perfect chance to meet fellow service-minded students and learn more about what we do.

description:
Meet our new E-Board, connect with fellow members, and get creative by making your own cute bracelet or phone charm. Whether you’re a seasoned crafter or just looking for a fun way to unwind,🌟 this is the perfect chance to kick off the semester with good vibes and new friends 🌟

Snacks will be provided!

name: Caffè Italiano: Fall Coffee Hour & Open House
description: Join us for espresso and biscotti to kick off our fall season of events! Drop by the Center between 12:30 and 3:45 PM to learn about our fall programs and scholarship opportunities, meet our staff, or just have a coffee (or tea) with us. We'll also be giving away Italian books and prizes! Open to all SBU students, faculty, and staff. A presto!

name: Hidden Treasures: Decorate Your Merch GBM
description:
Join Hidden Treasures for a session of creativity and personalization. Bring any merch of your choice — shirts, tote bags, hats, you name it — and decorate it your way. Whether you’re adding flair to an old favorite or creating a brand-new statement piece, this is your chance to make something uniquely yours. All decorating supplies will be provided — just bring your merch and your imagination! 

Snacks will be provided.

name: Roosevelt Field Day
description:
🎉 Roosevelt Field Day 🎉
📍 ERC / Roosevelt Pit
📅 Sunday, September 1st
🕓 4:00 PM – 6:00 PM

Join the Eleanor Roosevelt Community and close out the long weekend with an afternoon of fun and connection! Enjoy:

  • Exciting Games & Activities

  • Crafts & Music

  • Food & Refreshments

Bring a friend, and relax with us in the Pit. Free and open to all Roosevelt students—don’t miss it!

name: Red Watch Band Training *Homecoming Special with F&S Life*
description:
Join us for this Homecoming Special Red Watch Band co hosted by Fraternity and Sorority Life to learn how to identify an overdose and how to call for help to save a life!

 

You must register for this training at tinyurl.com/cpobystander

 

There will be free pizza for all attendees right after the training! 

 

 

To earn the Upstander Award (includes a certificate and graduation cord), you will need to complete the following: 

May 2026 graduates: complete Red Watch Band, QPR and Green Dot by January 1, 2026

May 2027 graduates: complete Red Watch Band, QPR and Green Dot by January 1, 2027

May 2028 graduates: complete Red Watch Band, QPR, Green Dot AND Hands-Only CPR by January 1, 2028

May 2029 graduates: complete Red Watch Band, QPR, Green Dot AND Hands-Only CPR by January 1, 2029

 

For accessibility needs please contact prevention_outreach@stonybrook.edu

name: Samosas & Mimosas
description: Enjoy board games, samosas, and mango juice, and hang out with SASA!

name: BSU's First Annual Kickoff
description: Bring a blanket to eat and play with the Black Student Union as we enjoy food and games at Staller Steps!! Greek Life will be strolling, and food will be provided. Meet the e-board and make new friends!

name: Pizza Party
description: Grab some pizza on Hillel while the cheese is still hot!! We'll be hanging out right across from SAC Plaza just handing out pizza shlepped all the way from Saulys in Hempstead. Hang out with new and old friends and enjoy the vibes.

💡 Ask your questions, get inspired, and learn where a future in STEM can take you. Don’t miss it! 🚀

name: Delta Sigma Pi - Informational #2
description: This event aims to share details and information about Delta Sigma Pi, its purpose, and the benefits that new members can gain from joining. We plan to share achievements, goals, plans, initiatives, and other important information while allowing new potential members to ask questions regarding the professional fraternity. In the second half of the event, we plan to allow the new potential members to network and meet the fraternity brothers

🌸 UASA Picnic at DeVries Patio! 🌸
Join us for a cozy afternoon filled with fun and good vibes! We’ll have games, arts & crafts, snacks, and refreshing drinks waiting for you. Come hang out with friends, enjoy the sun, and make some sweet memories! 🧃🍕🎨✨

name: Heat & Heat: Fire Safety and Prevention
description:
Fire prevention at Stony Brook University is a year-round effort by Fire and Emergency Services. During the month of September, we conduct numerous fire safety lectures, fire extinguisher training, and live-burn demonstrations in each of the residential communities and host an annual fire and safety expo on the academic mall. 

Join us and the first 300 participants will get a voucher for food and refreshment for the event.

description:
In the spirit of Thanksgiving, Actors Conservatory is hosting a Performance Club Friendsgiving! Come see some of your favorite performance clubs perform, support friends, and form connections between one another at this event! Even if you aren't a member of a club, or a member of a non-performance club, we extend this invitation to you :)

For the most up-to-date information about this event, follow our Instagram: @sbu_actorsconservatory

Light refreshments and games will be provided. NO REGISTRATION REQUIRED, JUST DROP IN!

name: Sunrise GBM
description:
Sunrise SBU GBMs are a weekly space where student climate activists, organizers, and new members come together to connect, plan, and build collective power to fight for climate justice at Stony Brook University, in New York State, and across the nation.

Sunrise SBU is a hub of the Sunrise Movement, a youth-led movement to stop climate change and create millions of good jobs through a Green New Deal. As a hub, we work to empower students to take action through grassroots organizing, political education, and coalition-building.

name: SBU Comp Prog Weekly Meeting
description:
Every week, we meet on Tuesday at 7pm in the Old CS Building Room 2114. At each meeting, we hold a practice contest on vjudge.com, and we have free pizza. 

Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club is a place for anyone interested in algorithms and programming. We offer lectures on algorithmic problems solving, host speakers to discuss algorithms, and provide training sessions for our ICPC teams.
The weekly contest takes place on vjudge.com, where participants are presented with a set of problems to solve within specified time and memory constraints. Solutions can be submitted in various programming languages, including C, C++, Java, and Python, among others. All students from SBU are encouraged to participate. We usually have a collaborative environment where everyone can seek assistance from each other. If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!

name: 💃🏽 Baile ❤️🇲🇽
description:
Come join us for a fun evening of music, culture, and dancing!
The Mexican Club will be hosting a baile (dance) event, where we’ll teach traditional Mexican dances.

name: Deep Learning Workshop: GBM #2
description: Take your AI skills to the next level with Deep Learning. In this hands-on workshop, you’ll explore how neural networks work, how to build them using modern frameworks, and how they’re applied in fields like computer vision and natural language processing. Ideal for anyone with basic ML knowledge looking to dive deeper into the power of deep learning.

name: Food for Thought
description:
Join us for a meaningful, creative, and fun event where we’ll learn about the health impacts of hunger and take action together! 💌💙

 

📚 What’s Happening?

  • Write & decorate uplifting cards for Meals on Wheels recipients ✨

  • Add kind words, colorful drawings, and stickers 💌🌸

  • Help brighten someone’s day while learning the importance of emotional support 💙

 

🍕🥤 Free Food & Snacks!
Enjoy FREE pizza, snacks, and drinks while you create. Come hungry, leave inspired.

 

👥 Space is Limited!
This event is capped at 22 participants, so don’t miss your chance to make an impact!

name: ULR GBM
description: Join the Undergraduate Law Review for a general body meeting! Get involved with writing and editing, explore legal scholarship, and gain tips and tricks for law school at our biweekly GBMs. We look forward to your involvement!

name: Guacamole Event
description:
💚🥑 Nothing brings people together like making guacamole together! Join MEXSO for a cozy night of fresh flavors, laughter, and comunidad ✨. We’ll be mashing up creamy avocados 🥑 with all your favorite fixings and a whole lot of cariño 💕—all hands-on, so you get to make your own guacamole!

Kick back with friends as we share music 🎶 and celebrate cultura together 🌟. This isn’t just guacamole—it’s a fun, interactive taste of home and community. 💚

📅 Date: October 1st
⏰ Time: 8pm–8:50 pm
📍 Location: SAC 305

name: GradConnect Series
description:
We are excited to invite graduate students to the first-ever GradConnect Series, organized by the Graduate School.

 

This new monthly series will partner with on-campus and off-campus resources to provide students with direct opportunities to learn about available services and connect. The event will also be a platform for graduate students to interact with each other, share experiences, and build a supportive community.

 

For the first GradConnect Series event, we are delighted to welcome the Graduate School leadership team, who will be there to interact with students, including;

 

Celia Marshik, Dean of the Graduate School and Vice Provost for Graduate Education

David A Rubenstein, Associate Dean for Academic and Student Affairs

Elizabeth Boon, Associate Dean of Strategic Initiatives

Christine Greenberg, Director of Student Services

 

Light refreshments will be provided.

 

 Plus, attend at least three of the four Fall 2025 events (September-December) in the GradConnect Series to be entered into our Grand Raffle Prize drawing in December!  Please stay tuned for more details and dates.

 

We hope you’ll join us for this exciting kickoff and take advantage of the chance to connect with both the Graduate School team and fellow graduate students.

name: Latino Night w/ RA Bryant & RA Shane
description:

For accessibility needs, or questions on the event, please email bryant.pena@stonybrook.edu and shane.patandin@stonybrook.edu in advance.

Fill out a form to show what you learned and be active in the fight against hazing! Meet in the Cardozo Lounge at 12:45pm on Tuesday, Sept. 23rd to walk over to the Union for the 1-3pm event!

name: Inspiring the Future: Latinos in Health Care | Faculty Panel and Meet & Greet
description:
Inspiring the Future: Latinos in Health Care
Faculty Panel and Meet & Greet

Wednesday, October 1st, 2025 | 6:00 PM –8:00 PM | UNITI Cultural Center - SB Union 113

Join us for a dynamic Health Professions Panel where SBU’s own Latino faculty from Stony Brook Medicine and the Health Sciences will share their journeys. 
This event is an opportunity to hear first-hand from accomplished professionals about the challenges and rewards of working in health fields, the importance of representation, and strategies for success.

Come meet the panel, ask your questions in a small, intimate setting to get you closer to achieving your dreams.

Panelists:

  • Carley Gomes, MD – Neonatology

  • Gloria Viboud, PhD, SM (ASCP) – Clinical Laboratory Sciences

  • Giselle Gerardi, PhD, RN, RNC-OB, C-EFM – School of Nursing

  • Maria E. Torres, PhD, MA – Associate Dean for Academic Affairs, School of Social Welfare

  • Rosalia Casanova, OTD, OTR/L – Occupational Therapy

This event is free and open to all students. Seating is limited. Refreshments will be served
Sponsored by Diversity, Intercultural and Community Engagement; Student Life at the Health Sciences Center; and the School of Health Professions.

name: Clubs & Orgs Leadership Council
description:
Student Clubs & Organizations are invited to send one executive board representative from their club to participate in a monthly leadership council! 

This month, we will be giving club leaders the opportunity to mingle and discuss club business, while also participating in an activity with an opportunity to have a funded campus lifetime program! 


Student leadership representatives will be able to speak on their club experience and provide feedback directly to Student Engagement & Activities. Together with our department, we hope to build and improve our processes & policies, as well as provide the most up-to-date information to all club leaders.

Light refreshments will be served!

name: Halloween Discoteka
description:
Get ready to turn up at Discoteka — the ultimate Halloween dance party on campus! 💃🕺 Step into the Shore Club for a night packed with music, energy, and nonstop vibes that’ll keep you moving all night long. ⚡

🔥 DJ Nic, one of Stony Brook’s top campus DJs, will be throwing down the beats — we’re bringing that Brighton Beach-style nightlife right to campus: flashy, fun, and just the right amount of chaos. 😎

Sip on spooky mocktails 🍹, grab some killer food 🍴, and load up on candy 🍬 to fuel your night. Don’t forget your costume — the most creative and spook-tacular fits will take home some epic prizes! 🏆👻

🎃 RSVP is required to get priority for food and treats — spots fill up fast, so don’t sleep on it!

Bring your crew, dress to impress, and get ready for a night full of lights, beats, and good vibes. 💥 This isn’t your average Halloween party — it’s Discoteka, and it’s gonna be legendary. 🚨

name: Hall-O-Jinn
description:
Pakistani Student Association is hosting Hallo-O-Jinn, a Halloween event that also highlights Pakistani culture. The goal is to create a fun and engaging space where students can share the supernatural creature stories and folktales many of us grew up hearing, along with personal experiences tied to them. It will be both entertaining and cultural, giving students the chance to connect, learn from each other, and build community in a relaxed setting.

This event will encourage interaction, allow students to celebrate Halloween with a cultural twist, and provide a unique way for people to meet and engage with each other.

Connect. Explore. Grow.

Join us for the CTI Major Meet-Up, an informal and welcoming event where you can connect with fellow students, major advisors, and professors. Whether you're still settling into your major or looking for guidance on what’s ahead, this is a great chance to build relationships and learn from those around you.

🎯 Who Should Attend:

1st and 2nd Year Majors in:
Chemistry | Engineering Chemistry | Biochemistry | Physics | Astronomy | Geology | Earth and Space Sciences | Business | Economics | Math

☕ What to Expect:

  • Bagels, snacks, and coffee to enjoy

  • Meet fellow students in your major

  • Connect with faculty and advisors

  • Start building your academic and professional network early

  • Hear advice and insights to help guide your path

  • Get to know the learning community around you

Come as you are—stay for the connections that can shape your journey!

name: Kevin Yager Guest Speech by Brookhaven National Laboratory
description:
Brookhaven National Laboratory: The Future of AI-Empowered Science

📆 Date: Monday, October 20

🕓 Time: 4:00 PM – 5:00 PM

📍 Location: SAC 302

🗣 Speaker: Kevin Yager, CFN Interim Director at BNL

Students will have the opportunity to hear from Dr. Kevin Yager, Director of the Center for Functional Nanomaterials (CFN) at Brookhaven National Laboratory and a leading voice in AI-driven scientific research.

Dr. Yager will discuss how cutting-edge artificial intelligence is transforming scientific discovery—from autonomous experiments to accelerated materials design—and what this means for the future of research and innovation.

The event will feature:

• 🎤 Talk: A presentation by Dr. Yager on the future of AI-empowered science

• ❓ Q&A: An open forum for student questions

• 🤝 Networking: A chance to personally speak with Dr. Yager after the session

• 🍕 Food: Pizza and soda will be served!

All students are welcome—no prior AI experience required.

name: BMES Senior Checklist
description: Hey! 👋 If you’re a BME major, no matter what year you’re in, you won’t want to miss this! 🎓 BMES is hosting a Senior Checklist session with BME Undergraduate Program Coordinator Jessica Kuhn on Monday, October 20th, 2025 from 12:30–2:00 PM in SAC 302! 🧠 Whether you’re trying to figure out which classes to take, how to stay on track for graduation, or looking for pre-med advice, this session is the perfect place to get all your questions answered. It’s a relaxed, informative event where you can plan ahead, clear up confusion, and make sure you’re setting yourself up for success in BME. 💙

name: 🧀 Queso & Questions❓
description:
Get ready for a burst of flavor and fun at Queso & Questions, the ultimate trivia balloon pop challenge! Here's how it works:
- 🎯Throw a Dart — Score enough points for the designated Balloon
- 🎈 Pop a Balloon — Each balloon hides a mystery question inside.
- 🧠 Answer It Right — Show off your smarts and tackle the trivia!
- 🎉 Win a Prize — Flip the paper to reveal your reward: every correct answer earns you the prize printed on the back!
From a nostalgic Tom & Jerry keychain set to some cool SBU Merch, every balloon holds a chance to win something exciting. Whether you're a queso connoisseur or a trivia master, this event is your chance to snack, laugh, and score big.

Join The Society of Professional Journalists, The Student Media Council and WUSB radio station for a podcast bootcamp to prepare you to submit for the National Public Radio podcast challenge in 2026. You have a chance to win a $5000 NPR prize plus prizes and give aways from Student Community Development's Student Media Service Office.

name: Holiday Paint Night
description: In this event, residents will paint holiday pictures to destress before finals! They meet new people and learn about other holiday traditions. Holiday themed cookies and candy will be provided, as well as canvases for artwork!

name: Voices y Vibras: Culture, Careers & Community
description:
Join us for an evening that celebrates culture, identity, and professional growth. The event opens with a Latiné Professional Panel, where speakers from fields like health, business, and education will share their career journeys and how their cultural backgrounds shaped their experiences.

After the panel, residents will take part in the We Are Yang Banner Activity by adding designs, colors, or symbols that represent their heritage. The completed banner will be displayed in Yang Hall as a showcase of our community’s diversity.

Empanadas (from Chico’s Tex Mex) will be provided.

name: Game Night at Benedict
description:
Get ready for the ultimate chill night at Benedict!
Join the Benedict Hall Council for a fun and competitive Game Night filled with poker, bingo, Uno, and more. Whether you want to play like a pro or just hang out with friends, this is the perfect way to unwind, meet new people, and enjoy good vibes.

 

We’ll have light background music, free snacks, and soft drinks (first come, first serve), so come early.

 

Feeling competitive?
Win rounds in our games to earn raffle tickets. The person with the most raffles at the end of the night will walk away with an OWALA Water Bottle – yes, the hype is real.

 

Everyone is welcome. Come to play, come to compete, or just come to vibe.
Bring your friends. Bring your energy. Bring your game face.

 

📅 4 December 2025
📍 Benedict South Lounge

⏰ 8 PM – 9 PM

Hope to see you all there!

name: SHWPS Seawolves Finals Market
description:
Friday, December 5th 12:30pm-2:30pm

Recreation Center Multipurpose Room

Finals Season can be stressful! Pick up health and wellness supplies & tips to stay healthy and don’t forget to grab some essential study supplies to thrive this finals season! 

FREE: Cold & Flu Kits, Ear Plugs, Highlighters, Note Cards, Pens, Pencils, Post-It Notes, DIY Stress Balls, Health & Wellness Resources

name: Sunrise GBM
description:
Sunrise SBU GBMs are a weekly space where student climate activists, organizers, and new members come together to connect, plan, and build collective power to fight for climate justice at Stony Brook University, in New York State, and across the nation.

Sunrise SBU is a hub of the Sunrise Movement, a youth-led movement to stop climate change and create millions of good jobs through a Green New Deal. As a hub, we work to empower students to take action through grassroots organizing, political education, and coalition-building.

name: Movie Night
description: Join us for a fun night movie night, snacks, and great company to end off the term and relax before finals

name: Cafecito & Creativity
description: Enjoy a warm cup of coffee, pan dulce, and customize your own mug with SHPE!

name: Yang Carnival Part III
description: Stop by the lobby for free popcorn and cotton candy!

name: Take To: Pathways to Medicine, Physician Panel
description: Come with RA Marcy-Hanne to PACE Empowerment's annual physician panel! We're bringing together an incredible group of physicians who will share their professional journeys, offer insight into the realities of medicine, and speak on critical topics such as health equity and career development.

name: LemonAid Station @ SDM
description: Lemonade stand distributing refreshments and snacks for students in the School of Dental Medicine

name: UASA - Ukrainian 101
description:
Ready to learn something new? Ukrainian 101 is a fun and interactive introduction to the Ukrainian language and culture, hosted by the Ukrainian American Student Association! Whether you're completely new or just curious, this event will cover the basics — from greetings and key phrases to cultural insights and traditions.

Expect an engaging time filled with learning, laughter, and a few surprises along the way. Come expand your worldview and connect with a vibrant culture!

name: Diwali Celebration
description:
Join us for a vibrant evening in celebration of Diwali, the Festival of Lights!
Enjoy delicious food and take part in a special DIY light lamp activity, inspired by the tradition of lighting diyas.
🎨 Decorate your own custom light lamp and take home a beautiful keepsake!

✨ All are welcome—come celebrate Diwali with us! ✨

🗓️ Date: Monday, Oct 20
📍 Location: SAC Ballroom B
⏰ Time: 7:00–9:00 PM

name: Diwali HSA x SASA
description: Join HSA and SASA for a night of food, games, and culture! You will have a chance to win prizes, get your mehendi done, paint diyas, and so much more. Watch exciting dance performances while enjoying pani puri, mithai, and chai, don’t miss it!
categories: Asian Pacific Islander Desi American Heritage Month

name: CASB: Fall Picnic
description: With classes beginning, join us at CASB's annual picnic to meet new people and make new friends! Enjoy a beautiful afternoon outside with CASB, playing team games, volleyball, free snacks, and more. Come by, join the fun, and let us get to know you!

name: Char Siu Night
description: Join us for an unforgettable evening filled with laughter, excitement, and mouthwatering flavors! Gather your friends for a lively night of fun games that will keep you entertained and on your toes with CTC! Between the rounds of friendly competition, indulge in our delicious, freshly prepared Cha Siu — savory, tender, and bursting with flavor. Whether you’re here for the games, the food, or the great company, this is the perfect way to spend your evening!

name: iCafe: Communication Tool Kit
description:
In our Communication Tool Kit workshop, we will review techniques to practice and understand effective communication skills. Through interactive discussion, exercises and videos, participants will learn new ways to successfully communicate with others. This event will be tailored specifically towards undergraduate international students, and lunch will be provided! 

Presented by Donna Buehler, Director of The Ombuds Office.

Do you want to obtain a PAID summer 2026 internship at a TOP company?

Are you interested in business, technology, engineering, communications, journalism, healthcare, or the legal field?

If you answered yes, then you do not want to miss this event!

Students will have an opportunity to learn about various paid internship opportunities at top companies across the nation from the following organizations:

  • T. Howard Foundation
  • MLT

Agenda:

  • Welcome
  • Panel Introductions & Discussion
  • 2 rounds of breakout sessions to learn more about the application process

LUNCH IS PROVIDED!

description:

Cool treats, colorful sidewalks, and fun conversations await. This event will take place at the entrance of Toscanini Hall on September 4th from 6:30 PM to 8:00 PM.

For accessibility accommodations, please call 631-632-6648 or email eliana.teshome@stonybrook.edu

description:
🎨 Painted Playback 🎶
Join us for a fun and creative night where music meets art! At Painted Playback, you’ll get to decorate your very own CD with paints, markers, and stickers—turning a simple disc into a one-of-a-kind piece of art. Whether you go for bold colors, cool patterns, or fun sticker vibes, it’s all about making it your own.

✨ All supplies are provided, just bring your creativity!

name: Club Social
description:
Student Engagement & Activities invites you to an evening of games, collaboration, and fun at our Clubs & Organizations Social on September 8th from 6:00 PM to 8:00 PM in SAC Ballroom A. 

🌟 What's in Store for You:

🔹 Games & Mingling: Play some games and meet your future club collaborators! 

🔹 Free Food: Enjoy snacks 

🔹 Prizes: Be prepared for a chance to win amazing prizes for your organization including VOUCHERS FOR YOUR CLUB TO USE!

🔹 Meet Our Leadership Team: Get to know our dynamic offices, and who to contact! We're here to support you.

🎉 Event Details:

  • Date: September 8th
  • Time: 6:00 PM - 8:00 PM
  • Location: SAC BALLROOM A

Don't miss out on this fantastic opportunity to connect, learn, and have a blast with your fellow club leaders. Mark your calendars, spread the word, and get ready for an event that will elevate your club's potential!

name: Colors of Community

This fun and inclusive event is open to everyone so bring your friends! Enjoy snacks and drinks while exploring the diversity within our community and compete for the chance to win exciting prizes. BE THERE OR BE SQUARE! Please be on time as we will start right on the dot! For any questions or concerns, please contact oyshe.khan@stonybrook.edu

name: RHA Fall Retreat 2025
description:
💖 RHA in the Dreamhouse: Fall 2025 Retreat 💖

Step into the Dreamhouse with the Residence Hall Association (RHA) as we kick off our Fall 2025 Retreat! This Barbie-themed day is all about welcoming and training new Hall Council members in true Dreamhouse style. 🌟

Get ready for engaging info sessions, delicious food, and a spirit activity to spark your creativity and teamwork. It’s the perfect chance to connect with RHA leaders and meet Hall Council members from other buildings! ✨

This retreat is mandatory for the Core Six members of each Hall Council. Don’t forget to RSVP! 💕

name: Family Weekend: Welcome on the Patio
description:
Join us at the East Side Patio for our Family Weekend Kick off!

Stony Brook University's Family Weekend is a prideful tradition; our team looks forward to the weekend lineup every year. Our programming is open to all undergraduate and graduate students, and their families. 

For more information about Family Weekend and a tentative schedule, click here!

**With the exception of CVENT events, All Family Weekend Events are First Come First Served. RSVPing on SBEngaged does not guarantee admission. There are multiple activities scheduled at the same time, and multiple iterations of some activities—we suggest having an idea of alternate events in the case that one is at capacity.**

name: Tea and Talk
description: A fun opportunity for our members to gather to enjoy some tea and Russian conversation!

name: Tea and Talk

name: Pokemon Go Trivia Night
description: Come join the Pokemon Go Club for our E-Board Introductions and a variety of games! This GBM will be centered around encouraging members to work together in a variety of games including Trivia and Drawing related games. Snacks will be served and prizes will be awarded.

name: Birdies Patchogue
description:
Golf Club Night at Birdies 🎉
Come join us for a night out at Birdies in Patchogue on Thursday, October 16th! ⛳️

Birdies is the perfect mix of golf and fun, featuring indoor golf simulators, great food, and a lively atmosphere. This event is a great way to relax, hang out with fellow Golf Club members, and enjoy some swings off the course.

Whether you’re new to the game or an experienced player, this will be a fun and social night you won’t want to miss! 🏌️‍♂️✨

name: SBU Comp Prog Weekly Meeting
description:
Every week, we meet on Tuesday at 7pm in the Old CS Building Room 2114. At each meeting, we hold a practice contest on vjudge.com, and we have free pizza. 

Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club is a place for anyone interested in algorithms and programming. We offer lectures on algorithmic problems solving, host speakers to discuss algorithms, and provide training sessions for our ICPC teams.
The weekly contest takes place on vjudge.com, where participants are presented with a set of problems to solve within specified time and memory constraints. Solutions can be submitted in various programming languages, including C, C++, Java, and Python, among others. All students from SBU are encouraged to participate. We usually have a collaborative environment where everyone can seek assistance from each other. If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!

name: Mooncakes & Marigolds
description: Join the Community Action Network for a cross-cultural holiday celebration! We will have mooncakes and paper lantern decorating for Mid-Autumn Festival, rangoli and diya painting for Diwali, marigold garland making and sugar skull painting for Day of the Dead, and "apple-bobbing", pumpkin pie, and candy for Halloween. There will also be many fun raffles and prizes to win throughout the night. Bring a friend, or make some new ones as you drink apple cider and make fun crafts, while all raising money for charity! You won't want to miss it!

name: SBU PSA Bake Sale!
description:
🍰 Bake Sale Alert! 🎉
Sweeten your day at the Union with the Pakistani Student Association’s Bake Sale! From rich gulab jamun cheesecake to classic brownies, cupcakes, creme brulee, tres leches, and more, there’s a treat for everyone. 🍫🍓🥮 Sip on a refreshing Shirley Temple while you indulge.

📍 The Union
📅 Thursday, Sept 18, 2025
⏰ 12:00 PM – 4:00 PM

Come hungry. Leave happy. 💚

name: Global CommuniTEA: Find your Flavor
description:
Join us for snacks and tea as we explore the power of culture and community in shaping our college experience.

This week we collaborate with the teaching kitchen and our campus dietician Laura Martorano.

Email prevention_outreach@stonybrook.edu for accessibility needs.

name: Advanced Python - Internal Competition Round 2
description:
Put your skills to the test in a fun, hands-on Reinforcement Learning challenge. Design and train AI agents to play Snake or solve a Rubik’s Cube—learning through trial and error, just like a human would. Compete with others to see whose model performs best, with $200 worth of prizes for the top performers. Whether you’re aiming to win or just want to experiment with RL, this is the perfect playground.

 

Round Details:

Round 1 - Intro to RL: It's a Me, RL! | October 15th, Frey 205

Round 2 - Advanced RL: Hungry Python | October 27th, SAC 302

Round 3 - Advanced RL: Car Racing RL Challenge | November 5th, Frey 205

*All rounds are from 12:30PM-1:50PM

name: Spooky Trick or Treating Walk with CPO
description:

Walk #1: Wednesday, October 8th from 5-6pm

All abilities are invited, no experience necessary, no one gets left behind

description: Learn about and get involved with blood cancer advocacy on campus! We have over 10 student organizations coming all dedicated to fighting cancer one way or another! Ranging from research, fundraising, activism, and community action, these groups do it all! Stop by and join the fight to eliminate blood cancers!

name: ACS: Professional Development Night

The ACS e-board will lead a presentation on building your resume, finding research opportunities, planning your coursework, and improving your interview skills. These tips and strategies are valuable for students of all majors and class years!

Need a break from classes and studying? Come hang out with RA Aina for a night of games, laughs, and good vibes! We’ll have board games for all types of players and a Nintendo setup for anyone ready to battle, or just have fun.

Game nights aren’t just about winning—they’re a great way to de-stress, recharge, and connect with people in the community.

📍 Where: Hand Hall Classroom

Snacks will be provided 🍿🥤—all you need to bring is yourself (and maybe your competitive side 😉).

Come for the games, stay for the laughs, and leave feeling refreshed. See you there!

description:
Join the Cardiology Student Association (CSA) and Stony Brook’s pre-health community for a thrilling night of fun, fright, and fundraising! The Haunted Health Fair combines Halloween festivities with interactive, health-themed games and activities hosted by various pre-health organizations across campus.

From dissecting “pumpkin hearts” and diagnosing “haunted patients” to crafting bubbling health potions and learning zombie first aid, this event puts a spooky spin on science and medicine! Attendees can enjoy festive music, candy, and photo ops, while competing in our costume contest and raffle giveaways for a chance to win exciting prizes.

All proceeds will support CSA’s heart-health awareness initiatives and collaborative community outreach efforts with other pre-health clubs. Don’t miss out on this one-of-a-kind experience that brings together students passionate about healthcare, education, and giving back, with a haunting twist!

description: Join RA Oyshe at the Hamilton Classroom for a movie night of Hidden Figures, the powerful true story of three women Katherine Johnson, Dorothy Vaughan, and Mary Jackson whose mathematical brilliance helped launch NASA’s space program while challenging racial and gender barriers in 1960s America. This movie night is more than entertainment, it’s an exploration of civic courage, representation, and the ongoing pursuit of equity. name: OpenAI x WebDev: Build an AI Interviewer
description:
Build an AI-Powered Technical Interviewer

Join us for a hands-on workshop where you’ll create a real-time, AI-driven interviewer that listens, analyzes, and responds — just like a real technical recruiter!

🧠 What You’ll Learn

  • 🎙️ Stream microphone input directly to GPT

  • ⚡ Process live responses for real-time interview simulation

  • 💻 Integrate with a coding environment for mock technical challenges

🧰 Tech Stack

Backend: FastAPI • OpenAI SDK • WebSockets
Frontend: Next.js (React) • TypeScript • Tailwind CSS
Dev Tools: Docker • Poetry • GitHub Codespaces (no local setup needed!)

🧩 Why You Should Join

  • Instantly spin up a browser-based development environment

  • Build a full-stack AI project from scratch

  • Learn modern tools for real-time GPT integration

  • 🍪 Snacks will be provided!

Whether you’re into AI, real-time systems, or modern full-stack dev, this workshop is for you.

👉 Come code with us and bring your interviewer to life!

description:
Join the Skincare & Wellness Club and Seawolves Accessibility Network for the first annual Living Textbook, an interactive event connecting individuals living with chronic illnesses and disabilities with listeners in an open dialogue about lived experience, accessibility, and patient-centered care. Volunteers who share their story will be paired with a small group of listeners for 15-20 minute discussions. Attendees can expect to come away with  a better understanding of the day-to-day experiences of individuals with chronic illness and disability that can't always be gathered from a textbook. 

Island Empanada catering will be provided.

Interested in sharing your story? Register here.

Alternate link: https://forms.gle/pkHEjJ4Sf8xLfrhD7

This event aims to spark meaningful conversation about Indigenous histories, storytelling, and representation in film. A brief discussion will follow the screening for those who wish to share reflections and insights.

Date: November 21, 2025
Time: 6:00PM- 8:30PM
Location: West MPR (Multi-Purpose Room)

Light refreshments and snacks will be provided.

If you require accessibility accommodations or have specific needs, please contact us at quenton.caine-jeffers@stonybrook.edu. We are happy to ensure everyone can fully participate and enjoy the event.

name: E&E Seminar: Evolutionary Ecology of Malaria Parasites in a Changing World
description:
Malaria has reshaped our world through its many victims, but how has it shaped its hosts?

Harvard researcher and disease ecology expert Benjamin L. Rice will present a fascinating talk, “Evolutionary Ecology of Malaria Parasites in a Changing World.” Malaria—one of the most influential diseases of the modern era—has shaped not only the lives of its victims but also the evolutionary trajectories of its hosts. Dr. Rice will explore the core evolutionary dynamics of these parasites and what they mean for our world moving forward. If any of this interests you, you won’t want to miss his talk!

⏰ When: Nov. 17 | 12:30 PM – 1:45 PM
🗺 Where: Laufer Center

name: Yalda Night
description:
Yalda Night (Shab-e Yalda) is one of the oldest and most cherished Iranian celebrations, marking the longest night of the year — the winter solstice. Rooted in ancient Persian traditions and Zoroastrian beliefs, it symbolizes the victory of light over darkness as days gradually begin to lengthen after the solstice.

We will gather to celebrate this night with some symbolic fruits such as watermelon (for health and longevity), pomegranates (representing rebirth and the cycle of life), and nuts and of coure persian food. These foods symbolize vitality, protection from illness, and the promise of light’s return. And the works of the Persian poet Hafez are especially recited.

description:

Students will be educated on various topics, such as how to complete voter registration forms, request absentee ballots, and more. Join us at our Thursday event to learn more about the election process. We will have fun games themed towards the election and its processes!

name: Create a Bouquet with CPA
description:
The College Panhellenic Association invites students to join us for a Bouquet Making Recruitment Event in collaboration with our three National Panhellenic Conference sororities on campus: Alpha Sigma Alpha, Sigma Delta Tau, and Theta Phi Alpha.
This event is designed to provide students with an opportunity to learn more about Panhellenic life, meet active members from each of the three sororities, and gain insight into the values, sisterhood, and experiences that Panhellenic membership offers. In a relaxed and welcoming setting, participants will create their own personalized floral bouquets to take home. The bouquet-making activity serves as both a fun creative outlet and a way to foster conversations and connections among students and sorority members.
The College Panhellenic Association (CPA) serves as the governing body for the Panhellenic sororities at Stony Brook University and is committed to promoting scholarship, leadership, service, and community among its members. This recruitment event reflects CPA’s mission of creating meaningful opportunities for students to engage with Greek Life while learning more about how to get involved on campus.
All students are welcome to attend, and no prior experience with crafts or knowledge of Greek Life is required. This event is open to anyone interested in exploring Panhellenic opportunities or simply looking to enjoy a hands-on activity while meeting new people

name: Sunrise GBM

Sunrise SBU GBMs are a weekly space where student climate activists, organizers, and new members come together to connect, plan, and build collective power to fight for climate justice at Stony Brook University, in New York State, and across the nation.

Sunrise SBU is a hub of the Sunrise Movement, a youth-led movement to stop climate change and create millions of good jobs through a Green New Deal. As a hub, we work to empower students to take action through grassroots organizing, political education, and coalition-building.

name: SBU Comp Prog Weekly Meeting
description:
Every week, we meet on Tuesday at 7pm in the Old CS Building Room 2114. At each meeting, we hold a practice contest on vjudge.com, and we have free pizza. 

Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club is a place for anyone interested in algorithms and programming. We offer lectures on algorithmic problems solving, host speakers to discuss algorithms, and provide training sessions for our ICPC teams.
The weekly contest takes place on vjudge.com, where participants are presented with a set of problems to solve within specified time and memory constraints. Solutions can be submitted in various programming languages, including C, C++, Java, and Python, among others. All students from SBU are encouraged to participate. We usually have a collaborative environment where everyone can seek assistance from each other. If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!

name: Book Launch: Giulia Riccò, The Italian Colony of São Paulo: Race, Class, and Cultural Capital in Brazil
description:
Dr. Giulia Riccò will present her new book, The Italian Colony of São Paulo: Race, Class, and Cultural Capital in Brazil, winner of the 2024 Aldo and Jeanne Scaglione Publication Award, MLA. This book argues that Italians first became racialized as white in São Paulo, Brazil, at the turn of the twentieth century. The book is available Open Access at Fordham Commons.

Dr. Riccò will be introduced by D’Amato Chair Dr. Loredana Polezzi, and following the presentation, Dr. Mary Jane Dempsey (LCS) and Simone Brione (English) will offer a response to the book.

Dr. Giulia Riccò is Assistant Professor of Romance Languages and Literatures at the University of Michigan, Ann Arbor. As a scholar of modern Italian and Brazilian literature who studies class, migration, and race, Dr. Riccò’s research addresses how the concept of Italian identity evolved beyond the strict geographical confines of the Italian nation-state.

name: Inclusion Is So Fetch!
description:
On October 3rd, he asked me what day it was… but on October 4th, you can join RA Tanim, RA Karina, and RA Dilsad to watch Mean Girls (2004) in the Tubman Multipurpose Room and discuss what it means to be inclusive in a world full of cliques and stereotypes. Enjoy an iconic movie while snacking on provided popcorn and chocolate! Let’s learn about how we can make our community one where everyone has a seat at the table. 

 

Come with an open mind (and in your most comfy pajamas)… because exclusion? She doesn’t even go here.

We hope you join us because you CAN sit with us! 😌❤️

While you watch, grab free popcorn & drinks 🥤🍿 and pick up our bite-size info pamphlets that unpack the psychology of watching movies—from why we love fear to how sound and lighting mess with our brains 🧠✨. The handouts are tied to PSY 103: Introduction to Psychology (a common SBC course for first-years), so it’s fun and a little academic-friendly. 📚

name: SBU Annual ICPC Selection Contest 2025
description:
SELECTION CONTEST ON OCT 10, 2025, with food and prizes!   See the attached flyer.

[ Please bring your own laptop, if possible ]

The Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club (CompProg) is organizing a local ICPC-style Coding Contest on

        Friday, October 10, 2025
        Room 2120 (with additional rooms as necessary) of Old Computer Science Building
        Contest hosted on HackerRank
        5:00 - 9:00 PM

Each participant must compete alone, and will get 7-8 algorithmic programming problems to solve in 3 hours.

The goal is to identify the top candidates for our International Collegiate Programming Contest(ICPC) teams, which will participate in the Greater New York Regional Contest in the second week of November for a slot in the ICPC World Finals of 2026.

Anyone with basic programming experience can take part and volunteers will help in explaining the format of the contest if you are not familiar with it. It is open to all SBU students.
 
We will try to provide computers to students without their own laptop but it is not guaranteed.

REGISTRATION:

Registration for the contest is open now. Please register at:

https://forms.gle/d3xMzK9RaL2h8Jep9
 
Since the contest will be hosted on HackerRank, you must register a free account at www.hackerrank.com (which takes about a minute) if you have not already done so. Note that for your HackerRank account you must use exactly the same email address and first/last names that you will provide in the contest registration form above.

SPONSOR:

Stony Brook Computer Science Department


PRIZES:

There will be prizes for the top 3 finishers in the selection contest for each category (9 prizes in total)

(1) Juniors (for first- and second-year undergraduates),
(2) Seniors (for third- and fourth-year undergraduates),
(3) Grads (for graduate students)


OTHER INFO:

For more information about ICPC, please check out http://www.acmgnyr.org.

Problem sets used in our old selection contests can be found here: https://www3.cs.stonybrook.edu/~compprog/.

We also have a Google group for competitive programming: https://groups.google.com/a/cs.stonybrook.edu/d/forum/compprog

Please join the group if you are interested in receiving future announcements. You can join our discord too. 

We look forward to seeing you at the contest!

--
Saajid Chowdhury
President
SBU CompProg
Website: http://cs.stonybrook.edu/~compprog/

 

To unsubscribe from this group and stop receiving emails from it, send an email to compprog+unsubscribe@cs.stonybrook.edu.

name: Naga-Night: Fireball Festival
description: The Naga Fireball Festival, or Bang Fai Phaya Nak, is an annual Thai event typically held in northeastern Thailand, where glowing red orbs rise from the Mekong River. Locals believe the fireballs are exhaled by the Naga, a mythical river serpent. In celebration of the Naga Fireball Festival, Baan Thai Club is hosting its own "fireball" festival. This event will have performances featuring other student organizations while our general body can participate in activities and fun games hosted by BTC. Food will also be catered from Raan Thai.

description: Curious about how to get started in research as an undergraduate but don't know where to start? Join us for an engaging panel discussion in collaboration with the Stony Brook iGEM Team featuring fellow undergraduates and recent grads who are actively involved in clinical/translational, molecular biology, chemistry, bioinformatics, psychology, and biomedical engineering research. Our panelists will share their personal experiences regarding how they found their respective opportunities, what their day-to-day looks like, the challenges they’ve faced, and the skills they’ve gained along the way. Whether you’re just starting out or looking to explore new opportunities, this is your chance to learn and connect! There will also be FREE pizza and snacks

Join us for a night of fun filled with games, crafts, and yummy FREE snacks!!! Come decorate your own lanterns and release them on water to take some magical photos! Don't miss out on this amazing photo opp and a fun time to meet new friends and get to know FCC as well! See you there!!

name: Dia de los Muertos | Day of the Dead Celebration
description:
Dia de Los Muertos | Day of the Dead Celebration
Monday, November 3rd, 2025
7:00-9:00 PM | Student Activities Center, Ballroom A

Join us as we honor and celebrate Día de Los Muertos (Day of the Dead) — a vibrant cultural tradition that pays tribute to the lives and memories of our loved ones who have passed. Although Dia de los Muertos is celebrated throughout Latin America, it has deep pre-colonial indigenous roots.

Guests can take part in traditional crafts, including papel picado, mask painting, and tissue paper flowers, and are invited to contribute photos or small offerings to a community ofrenda (altar) in remembrance of family, friends, or cultural icons who have inspired them. 

This celebration will feature a special workshop led by the Calpulli Mexican Dance Company. Come early to sign up for the limited workshop spots.

Enjoy Latin American refreshments as we come together to celebrate life, legacy, and community through art, dance, and shared stories.  
This event is free and open to all.

Sponsored by:

  • Hispanic Heritage Month Committee (HHM)
  • Diversity, Intercultural and Community Engagement (DICE)
  • Latin American and Caribbean Studies Center (LACS)
  • Department of Hispanic Languages and Literature (HLL)
  • Mexican Student Organization (MEXSO)
  • Latin American Student Organization (LASO)
  • International Student Organization (ISO)

name: International Education Week: International Game Night
description:
You’re invited to International Education 2025: “Rooted in Culture, Growing in Community!” 

In celebration of International Education Week, the International Student Center is hosting Global Game Night!

Join us for an evening of learning and playing board games from around the world, snacks, and fun! 🎮

name: CSTEP Match Presents: Turning your Service into Impact with Alissa Moeller
description: Join us for CSTEP Match Presents: Alissa Moeller on Friday, October 24 from 12:30–1:30 PM in Old Comp Sci 1309. Alissa Moeller, Community Program Outreach Coordinator, will share how to turn service experiences into meaningful opportunities for growth, leadership, and impact. Learn how to connect your passions to community work, build relationships through service, and transform your experiences into valuable skills. Snacks will be provided—don’t miss out!

name: Registration Carnival
description:
The Undergraduate Colleges is hosting a Registration Carnival for the Winter and Spring 2026 semesters! Join us in the Union Ballroom (2nd floor) from 1:00-4:00pm for in-person drop-in advising appointments, carnival games, snacks and giveaways.

You can meet in-person with an advisor to get your registration questions answered, talked about major changes, and more. Plus, we'll have carnival games, giveaways and snacks for you to make it worth your while. Check-in for an appointment on Navigate to be seen by an advisor!

address.address: 250 John S. Toll Drive, Stony Brook, New York
address.name: Union Ballroom, 2nd Floor

name: NRHH GBM #4: Cocoa for Courage
description:
Join NRHH for our biweekly GBM as we write letters for the Fire and Emergency Services team. We’ll also give out hot chocolate as we write letters. A warm drink to compliment a heartwarming letter for people that keep campus from being ablaze! There'll also be committee introductions by our E-Board members for our general body members to pick from and be a part of to make NRHH stronger than ever this school year!

Meeting starts at 7PM on October 16th in the Student Activities Center Room 304! See y'all there :)

name: Trick or Trivia
description: STOP BY FOR JEOPARDY STYLE TRIVIA AND DISCUSSION ABOUT POLITICS AND INTERNATIONAL POLICIES. WITH PRIZES AND HALLOWEEN SNACKS

name: Ice Rice Baby
description:
❄️🍚 ICE RICE BABY 🍚❄️
Get ready to chill out and turn up with us at one of the most exciting events of the semester! 🧊✨

Come meet and celebrate our brand new reps as they take the stage with a special choreographed dance performance 💃🕺 — and that’s just the start! We’ll be serving up delicious ice rice 🍧, hosting fun themed games, and giving away cool prizes 🎁 throughout the night.

Expect performances, laughter, competition, and lots of good vibes as we officially welcome our newest faces to the fam 💙

So grab your friends, bring your energy, and don’t miss out — it’s going to be an ice rice night to remember! ❄️😎

name: Ecology and Evolutions Society Club: Trend & Drivers of Marsh Migration
description:
If you are a costal enthusiast you won't want to miss this Eco Evo Society GBM with Guest Speaker Sixto Taveras Lopez. In this talk Sixto will explain the process of Marsh Migration and how this combats rising sea levels on Long Island's shores. 

He will also dive into analytical careers in Ecology and Evolutions and how E&E professionals use technology to gather environmental data.

If all that interests you, join us in Room 105 for a fun journey through Long Island's shores

There will also be Free Food and fun Games!

address.address: 105 Life Sciences Building, Stony Brook, NY
address.name: Room 105

name: Portuguese Language Club: Hora do Cafezinho
description: "Hora do Cafezinho" or "Coffee Time" represents a Brazilian tradition of having coffee or tea with friends or family in the afternoon. We host this event to introduce our members to the Brazilian culture.

name: Bagel Bash!
description: Join Stony Brook Hillel's weekly Bagel Bash! Every Wednesday between 12:30 PM and 2:00PM Hillel is happy to host a delicious. and fresh Bagel Brunch, bring your friends and enjoy a delicious time!

name: Resume BINGO
description: Bring your Resume and learn tips to make it better while playing Bingo! Play and win prizes!

address.address: 225 Circle Road, Stony Brook, New York
address.name: Chavez MPR

name: Midterm Mindfulness Cafe
description: Take a mindful break from midterms at the Midterm Mindfulness Café! Sip hot cocoa, paint your own wooden initials, and enjoy snacks with friends. You’ll also get a midterm de-stress kit to help you recharge and refocus for the rest of the week!!

name: SASE Decoden
description:
✨ DECODEN CRAFT NIGHT ✨
Get creative with SASE and design your very own custom compact mirror or card sleeve using cute charms, pearls, and decorations! 🎀 Bring your friends, relax, and enjoy free snacks while crafting something totally unique.

🧋 Come for the vibes, stay for the creativity — don’t miss out! 💖

name: Matcha and Memories
description:
Sip. Paint. Frame.

Unwind with a cozy evening of matcha and creativity! Join us in the Hand Hall Classroom to enjoy a soothing cup of matcha while you decorate your own picture frame using paints, gems, and other fun materials.

No experience needed—just bring your creativity and good vibes!

🗓️ Date: Wednesday, November 12, 2025
🕕 Time: 6:00 PM
📍 Location: Hand Hall Classroom
🎨 All supplies provided
🍵 Free matcha served
🎁 Frames available first come, first serve

Come early to grab your frame and create something beautiful to take home!

name: 🍁 Noche de Gratitude 🇲🇽🧡
description:
Before heading home for Thanksgiving break, join us for a cozy night of reflection, appreciation, and creativity! 🧡✨

As our last MEXSO meeting of the semester, we’ll celebrate the strong sense of familia that runs deep in Mexican culture — because gratitude and togetherness are at the heart of who we are. 💖

We’ll be making thank-you cards to gift to family or friends, sharing memories, and giving thanks for the community we’ve built this semester. 🫶

📅 Date: Nov. 17th
📍 Location: SAC Loft
🕕 Time: 8pm-10pm

Come for the vibes, stay for the familia. 💌🌮

name: Be the Voice of Your Generation: A FirstGen Panel & Brunch
description:
Join us for a delicious brunch and an inspiring conversation with FirstGen faculty, staff, administrators, and students, with real stories, honest advice, and practical tips for navigating college as a first-generation student. Whether you're seeking guidance or simply want to feel part of a community that understands, this event is for you.

We are excited to have our panelist share their journey and wisdom. 

Dr. Celia Marshik | Dean of the Graduate School and Vice Provost for Graduate and Professional Education
Dr. Ryan Braun | Assistant Provost for Academic Advising, Division of Undergraduate Education

Giselle Castro | Student, School of Social Welfare
Isabelle Irizarry-McAdam | Student, Biochemistry major and Theatre Arts minor
Cenia Larios | Student, School of Social Welfare
Saniya Lopez | Student, Business Management and Pre-Law, Resident Assistant and Peer Career Coach
Jose Romero-Diaz | Student, School of Social Welfare

Also, stop by the various resource tables to connect with SB offices and learn the ways you can find support as a First-Generation College Student.

For more information and to see more events, visit stonybrook.edu/firstgen

name: ASA PRESENTS: ASIAN NIGHT
description: Bored on campus? Come out to Asian Night, our biggest event of the fall semester. There will be snacks, drinks, and breathtaking performances from members from our Stony Brook Community. The night will be pack with showcases of talents and a secret guest performer so make sure you don't miss it!! Mark you calendars for Friday, November 14th in the Sac aud as this is a spectacle you don't want to miss. Tickets will be available through our Instagram so claim you spot!!

name: Neural Networking Night
description:
AI Community at SBU invites YOU 🫵 to Neural Networking Night on November 21st, from 4:30PM to 6:30PM at SAC 302!

⭐️ Connect with students from every major through bingo, team challenges, board games, and more! Don't miss out on the chance to win a PANDA LEGO SET! 🐼

⭐️ Enjoy a relaxed social atmosphere with great conversations and fun activities. And yes, there will be FREE FOOD catered by O Sole Mio, so come hungry. 🍽️ 🍝

⭐️ This event is open to everyone! Bring your friends and meet new ones!

name: H-Community Fall Fest 🍂
description: Celebrate the season of fall and wellness with H-Community! Take a mindful break and enjoy cupcake decorating, keychain designing, pumpkin painting, raffles, and more! Learn about campus resources through tabling by the Center for Prevention and Outreach, the Campus Dietitian, and the Inter-Fraternity Council.

name: Thanks For Giving
description: Bring in non-perishables free food! Vibe with CSO and play games.

name: Sentiment Analysis Workshop
description: Elevate your AI skills by learning how to use sentiment analysis to analyze real world data. In this workshop, we'll guide you through how to create a sentiment analysis model to grade feedback submitted by students throughout the semester.

name: Resume to RA
description: Join RA Ryan in preparation and reviewing for the RA application. Going over key skills to highlight, Resume Tips, Interview advice, and the process of the RA application. Ending with a RA panel to ask questions regarding the role. Free snacks provided

address.address: Stony Brook, NY 11794, Stony Brook, NY

name: Living World Lecture Series: "The Afterlife of Roundup: Soil, Earthworms, and Plant Health"
description:
🌍 Living World Presents: The Afterlife of Roundup — Soil, Earthworms, and Plant Health 🌱

The world beneath our feet is alive — teeming with hidden communities and remarkable resilience. In our next Living World event, Dr. Sharon Pochron will reveal how even the soil itself tells a story of survival and adaptation.

Join us as Dr. Pochron shares her research on the effects of Roundup and explores what happens beneath the surface in “The Afterlife of Roundup: Soil, Earthworms, and Plant Health.” Discover how soil organisms respond, recover, and continue to shape the ecosystems we depend on.

📅 Date: Monday, November 17
🕕 Time: 6:00 PM

Come uncover the fascinating fight for life happening right under your feet!

name: CSTEP Sophomore Workshop: Can you Talk the Talk
description: CSTEP will be collabing with the Career Center to discuss tips and tricks on public speaking. Special guest Kimberly Bell from CELT will be sharing her advice for us. Stay till the end to win raffle prizes! Light snacks and refreshments will be provided.

name: Professor's Soapbox #2: Joseph Labriola & Ankita Nagpal
description: Join us and listen to professors from Stony Brook's Creative Writing Department speak about a topic of their choosing, followed by a Q&A session.

name: Waffles & Write-Ins
description: Join RA Aneesah to learn how to contact your elected officials about issues YOU care about, all while enjoying a tasty waffle brunch!

name: Tips, Tricks & Treats: RA Edition
description:
Curious about the RA application process? Stop by “Tips, Tricks & Treats: RA Edition” to learn helpful strategies for preparing a strong application.

This casual session will cover:

  • Practical tips and advice for your RA application

  • Guidance on interviews and essays

  • Information on what RAs do and how the application process works

🍫 Snacks and treats provided!
💬 Questions welcome — you can ask anonymously if you prefer

Whether you’re considering applying or just want to learn more about the RA role, this session is a chance to get guidance and feel prepared for the application process (DUE NOVEMBER 19, 2025 !!!)

name: Take a PAWS Before Finals with SASC & CPO!
description:
🐾 Take a Paws Before Finals! ☕🎁
Feeling the end-of-semester stress? Come unwind at PALS x SASC’s Finals De-Stress Event!

Join us for a cozy afternoon featuring:
✨ Build-Your-Own Finals Care Packages
🎄 Holiday Decoration Station
🧸 Sensory Area to relax and recharge
☕ Hot Chocolate Bar
🐶 And of course — PALS pups for cuddles and comfort!

📍 Student Union – COLA Lounge
🗓 Thursday, December 4th | 12 PM – 3 PM
Hosted by SASC & CPO

Take a moment to breathe, connect, and treat yourself before finals week!
For accessibility accommodations, please email sasc@stonybrook.edu

Come join the O'Neill Hall Council at our general body meetings! If you have any ideas or suggestions for our hall or any future events, come join us on Tuesdays from 8-9 pm.

Tonight we are playing Jeopardy!!!! There will be free snacks like brownies and cookies! We will also have awesome prizes!!!
