name: GSO JUMP START LUNCH
description:
Join us for the GSO Jumpstart Lunch!
Come meet fellow graduate students, enjoy free lunch, and learn about upcoming opportunities and events from the Graduate Student Organization. It’s a great chance to connect, share ideas, and take a break from your busy week!

name: GBM 8: Destress Before Finals!
description: Destress before finals! Enjoy pizza and create friendship bracelets for the SB Children's Hospital. This event will count for both GBM attendance and a community engagement point.

name: Never FELT Better! 🧵🧸✨
description:
🧵✨ Never Felt Better! ✨🧸

Destress and get creative before finals and make your own keychains 🔑, plushies 🧸, magnets 🧲, and more!

Snacks included 🍪!

📍 Irving Lounge
📅 Thursday, December 4th
⏰ 8:00 PM

name: Gratitude and Glitter
description: Celebrate the season of gratitude with PAL Riya and PAL Nairika! Join your fellow Seawolves for a relaxing evening of crafting thank-you notes and making cute jewelry to share with friends, mentors, or anyone you’re thankful for. All supplies will be provided — just bring your creativity and good vibes! Take a break from classes, make something meaningful, and spread a little Thanksgiving cheer across campus.

name: Smash Bros. Ultimate x Schomburg-West Tournament Night
description:
Take a break from Finals studying to get competitive with West!  Play Smash Bros. Ultimate in either a freeplay or tournament-style match.  Pizza is provided.  The tournament is duo-style.  The winners will get matching Mario and Luigi plush toys!

Here is the itinerary for the night:

Freeplay 7:00pm-8:00pm
Tourney 8:00pm-9:30pm
Freeplay 9:30pm-10:00pm

E-mail ava.green@stonybrook.edu for any accessibility concerns.

address.name: Multipurpose Room (Located in West E Commons)

name: Turkish Movie Night GBM
description:
Join us for a Turkish Movie Night GBM hosted by the Turkish American Student Association (TASA)! 🎬✨

We’ll be watching a popular Turkish film together in a relaxed and fun atmosphere, perfect for unwinding mid-week and connecting with fellow students interested in Turkish culture.

Enjoy sunflower seeds and Coke, just like a traditional Turkish movie experience!

📍 Location: SAC 302
📅 Date: Wednesday, November 19
⏰ Time: 6:00–7:00 PM

Whether you're familiar with Turkish movies or watching for the first time, everyone is welcome. Come hang out, enjoy good vibes, and spend an hour with us!

name: Open Mic Night!
description: Join Music and Medicine for an awesome open mic night! Each group will have the opportunity to perform songs they have been working on throughout the semester. After all the groups have gone, the stage is open to anyone! Enjoy some fun performances, maybe even perform yourself, and munch on some free food and drinks!

name: Diversity Decorating & Gingerbread! Decorate and Win Free Gingerbread Houses
description:
Join RA Jhey and the CAN club at Irving Lounge to decorate gingerbread men and take home some free decorations! There will be free food (soup), hot cocoa, and chances to take home your very own gingerbread house for free!! 
This event will be at the Irving Lounge on Wednesday December 03, 2025 at 8PM. Attendance is free, the decorations are free, and the food + hot cocoa is free too! Bring your friends and yourselves to destress from this semester! 

A competition will be held where the best decorated gingerbread men winners get free gingerbread houses to take home, but there will also be a raffle for gingerbread houses, wherein all proceeds will go to charity (this raffle is completely optional).

name: Movie Night at Benedict
description:
Ready for a cozy night with good vibes, good people, and free food? Come hang out with Benedict Hall Council for Movie Night in the North Lounge!

You get to vote for the movie, chill with friends, enjoy some laughs, and of course… grab FREE Domino’s pizza and drinks while you watch. 🍕🥤

Whether you want a break from classes, need a fun night with your suitemates, or just want free food (we don’t judge), this is the perfect place to be.

📅 Thursday, November 20th
⏰ 8 PM to 9 PM
📍 Benedict North Lounge

name: Let It Go: Plate Smashing Stress Relief!
description: Join RA Lauren in a viral stress relief trend! Write all your worries on a plate and then smash it!! Safety equipment and free snacks provided! RSVP as we have limited plates.

name: Karaoke & Matcha Night!
description:
Join Chavez Hall Council and friends as we sing our hearts away while relaxing over matcha, diet soda and drizzilicious snacks!

Wednesday December 3rd, 6-8PM, in the Chavez MPR!!

Make this event your chance to relax and feel empowered to ace your finals!

 

(Free 8oz glass jars will be given away while supplies last!)

(We will have Whole milk and Oat milk while supplies last!)

For accessibility concerns, accommodations, or questions please contact Derek Lucido, @ 347-339-4580.

name: Holiday Kickback
description: Join us for a relaxed holiday study session with snacks, drinks, and fun activities!!

name: Cocoa and Charms
description:
Join PALs Piyal, Kirsten, Brandon, and Varun for Cocoa and Charms! Get cozy and creative at our Hot Cocoa and Decoden night! Decorate your own phone case, mirror, or jewelry box with adorable charms and cute designs, and enjoy some hot cocoa and snacks. It's the perfect mix of cozy and crafty!

📍 Where: The Pit Stop Commuter Lounge!

📅 When: December 1st from 5:30 PM to 7:30 PM

⭐ Perks!: Free Stuff, Free Food, Hot Cocoa, Cookies, meet some new people, and bring your friends!

name: Winter Wonderland
description: This is a casual event to wind down before finals season starts. We'll be relaxing with hot chocolate and marshmallows along with other tasty treats! There will also be board games. Hopefully we can brighten up everyone's end to the semester! To help with that, we'll also give everyone goody bags!

name: The Campus Residences First Annual Tree Lighting
description:
We are delighted to invite you to a very special occasion: The Campus Residences First Annual Tree Lighting! We are so excited to inaugurate this event as a vibrant new tradition that brings the Stony Brook community together. 

It will be a festive, fun gathering for students and staff alike on Wednesday, December 3rd, from 4:00 PM to 6:00 PM. We are located at 100 Circle Road, between Irving and O'Neill Halls (In front of the former Camp Res Main Office-Circle Road parking lot side). 

We will have:

• A raffle to win fantastic merch

• Hot chocolate from Kilwin’s

• S'mores

• Music

• A visit from Wolfie

• Giveaways

• The official lighting of the tree 

• Special appearance from our Stony Brook Women's & Men's Basketball Teams, who will be signing posters

Please bring a non-perishable food donation for the SBU Food Pantry to help those in need.

We hope you will join us for this new community celebration. Your presence will help warm the start of the holiday season and serve as a wonderful testament to the collaborative spirit within the Division of Student Affairs.

address.address: 100 Circle Road, Stony Brook , New York
address.name: Between Irving and O'Neill Halls (In front of the former Camp Res Main Office-Circle Road parking lot side)

name: Community @UNITI
description:
Take a mid-week pause with us at Community @UNITI: Open Mic Night! 🎤☕🍩

We’re bringing together cocoa, donuts, and a room full of people ready to laugh, listen, and lift each other up. Share a joke, tell a story or just settle in and enjoy the energy, however you show up is perfect!

And yes, we’ve got a raffle with some seriously cute prizes:

✨ 3D Night Light (Star Wars themed)
✨ Reversible Plushie
✨ LEGO Roses

Everyone who comes gets a sticker pack too, a tiny thank-you for being part of the moment!

📍 UNITI Cultural Center
🗓 December 3rd, 2025
🕖 7:00 PM – 9:00 PM

Come hang out, create a little joy, and be part of a space that always feels like community. 💛

name: Game Night
description: Gershwin Hall Council will be hosting a Game Night Tournament. We will be providing pizzas alongside some video games for people to come and compete in.

name: Art and Mindfulness
description:
Join us for a relaxing session that blends art and meditation. This practice offers a space to unwind, reflect, and explore the present moment through sacred patterns and guided drawing activities, all while enjoying warm tea and light tea biscuits.

Activities include:

Printed Mandalas & Yantras Coloring – Relax as you color traditional sacred designs using colored pencils, markers, or crayons.

Guided Mandala Drawing – Create your own mandala for centering and self-reflection with pens, markers, or colored pencils.

Opposite-Hand & Eyes-Closed Drawing Challenges – Playful exercises to release perfectionism, spark intuition, and deepen mindfulness using the same art materials on paper.

No art experience needed, all skill levels are welcome!


Materials, tea, and tea biscuits will be provided.

name: Roller Hockey de-stress event
description: The team and others are invited to join us for finals de-stressing with a movie and pizza in the multipurpose room (MPR).

name: Family Weekend
description:
Family Weekend will span from Friday the 26th to Sunday the 28th. It's going to be a great weekend with plenty of opportunities to experience campus life with your student, including rooting for the Seawolves in a home football game, winning prizes at BINGO with the Deans, and a breakfast with the President!

Stony Brook University's Family Weekend is a prideful tradition; our team looks forward to the weekend lineup every year. Our programming is open to all undergraduate and graduate students, and their families. 

For more information about Family Weekend and a tentative schedule, click here!

**With the exception of CVENT events, All Family Weekend Events are First Come First Served. RSVPing on SBEngaged does not guarantee admission. There are multiple activities scheduled at the same time, and multiple iterations of some activities—we suggest having an idea of alternate events in the case that one is at capacity.**

name: Family Weekend: Murder Mystery Party
description:
Join Student Engagement and Activities for Murder Mystery Party on September 27th in SAC Ballroom A.

There will be Dinner provided!

RSVP is required for this event, please complete the form here: Family Weekend Murder Mystery.

This event is intended for socializing with attendees in small groups. Moderate stimulation possible.

Day of the Event: Upon arriving at the event, staff will ask you for your event pass to scan. This can be found in the CORQ app under the drop-down tab once you sign-in to CORQ using your Stony Brook NetID. This is a contactless check-in system.

For accessibility needs please contact sea_programsandtraditions@stonybrook.edu


***RSVPing on this page does not guarantee admittance if the event is full, it just reminds you of the event***

KBS Dance Team is hosting our END OF SEMESTER FIESTA! Come to the party to eat and bond over K-Pop! We are featuring games, prizes, and food! 

Everyone is allowed to enjoy the event but only the first 100 attendees are able to get food, so don't forget to come early if you want the free food!

Waitlist Process

Curious about Chinese and Asian-American culture, and want to learn about different ways to get involved within CASB and cultural orgs on campus? Join us for our First GBM and meet our cabinet members while enjoying various activities and free Chinese food!

All students are welcome, regardless of their background!

For any updates and questions, you can find us at:

FB: fb.com/casbstonybrook

IG: @stonycasb

TikTok: @stonycasb

name: Ice Cream Social

🍦🎨 MSA Ice Cream & Kahoot Night! 🎉
Kick off the semester with some sweet vibes! Join us in the first week of classes for a night full of ice cream, laughs, and friendly competition 🏆✨. Meet new friends, reconnect with old ones, and brush up on your islamic knowledge in a fun kahoot to win prizes!

name: Grooves and Games Night
description: Have a fun time, meet our club, and get hyped for the upcoming semester with free energy drinks! Enjoy free snacks and drinks while playing games such as Uno, Jenga, and Kahoot! Also sing your favorite songs at karaoke! This event is open to all students and free of charge. No jazz or musical experience necessary! Just come and enjoy a chill night before your first day of classes!

name: BizBites: Get a Taste of Your Career Future
description:
Register on Handshake!

Get a head start on your career journey with this opportunity to connect with the Career Center's Business Career Coaches. Enjoy a casual and fun welcome event where you can mingle with Career Center staff, learn about the incredible resources available to you, and get answers to all your career-related questions – all while enjoying some delicious bites! Whether you're curious about internships, resume building, or exploring career paths, BizBites is the perfect place to start. Don't miss this chance to kick off your college experience strong and set yourself up for future success!

name: Black & Latinx Student Involvement Fair
description:
The Black & Latinx Student Involvement Fair is a vibrant showcase of 60 student organizations including social, cultural, pre-professional, performance, fraternity & sororities, graduate and undergraduate groups, etc.  Explore information for campus resources and meet your campus student leadership that support and celebrate the Black and Latinx communities at Stony Brook University. This annual tradition creates a welcoming space for students to connect with peers, learn about involvement opportunities, and build a sense of community on campus.

 📅 Date: Wednesday, September 17, 2025
🕖 Time: 7:00 PM – 10:00 PM (Set-up begins at 6:00 PM)
📍 Location: SAC Ballroom A
💲 Cost: Free and open to all SBU students

All SBU students are invited to join us for an evening of networking, music, and cultural celebration while discovering ways to get engaged and make an impact at SBU.  The event will feature:

🍦 ICE Cream Bar
🎧 Live DJ
📸 360° Photobooth
🎭 Organization Showcase & Performances
🎁 3 Back-to-School Basket Giveaways

If your group would like to participate, please sign up using this link:
Black & Latinx Involvement Fair CLUB & ORG Sign-Up Form

description:
Due to inclement weather, this event has been rescheduled for the planned rain date of Sunday, September 14, 2025. All other details will remain the same. 

The Concorso will now be held on Sunday, September 14, 2025. Join the Center for Italian Studies for its XIX annual Dr. Robert D. Cess Concorso d'Eleganza, a celebration of Italian automotive excellence. Display vehicles will rally on Stony Brook University campus with viewing free and open to the public. We will feature classic and modern vehicles from Alfa Romeo, De Tomaso, Ducati, Fiat, Ferrari, Lancia, Maserati, and more.

name: Berry Good For You- Chocolate Strawberry Wellness Night
description: Come join us at Mount Hall on September 2nd Tuesday Night at 7pm.  A lot of us think wellness means strict dieting or cutting out fun, but really, it’s about balance. Chocolate-covered strawberries give us the best of both worlds: something nutritious, something sweet, and a mindful break with friends. Come try dipping strawberries into different flavors and learning about healthy eating.

name: Pre-Health APAMSA: Health Insurance 101
description: Health insurance may feel complicated due to the various plans and benefits that are covered, but it plays a crucial role in ensuring our access to health services. Join us at our interactive workshop as we break down how health insurance works, highlight resources available, and discuss why it is especially important for the AAPI community. Walk away with practical tips that can help you (and your family) navigate healthcare with confidence.

description:
Join us for the second annual Garba Night. It is going to be an unforgettable celebration that you will not want to miss! All are welcome to partake in a vibrant evening filled with delicious food, lively dancing, and lots of fun. Enjoy captivating performances and immerse yourself in the spirit of Navratri as we come together as a community.

This is a wonderful opportunity to connect with Chapin staff, IGSA, and Center for Indian Studies and to learn more about our rich traditions. Whether you're a seasoned dancer or new to the festivities, there's something for everyone.

Don't miss out on this chance to celebrate with us—bring your friends for a night of joy and unity! We can't wait to see you there!

name: Sunrise GBM
description:
Sunrise SBU GBMs are a weekly space where student climate activists, organizers, and new members come together to connect, plan, and build collective power to fight for climate justice at Stony Brook University, in New York State, and across the nation.

Sunrise SBU is a hub of the Sunrise Movement, a youth-led movement to stop climate change and create millions of good jobs through a Green New Deal. As a hub, we work to empower students to take action through grassroots organizing, political education, and coalition-building.

Bring your friends and family for a memorable weekend performance and show your support for dance! Snacks will also be provided!

We can’t wait to share this exciting showcase with you! 🍁✨

name: SBU Comp Prog Weekly Meeting
description:
Every week, we meet on Tuesday at 7pm in the Old CS Building Room 2114. At each meeting, we hold a practice contest on vjudge.com, and we have free pizza. 

Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club is a place for anyone interested in algorithms and programming. We offer lectures on algorithmic problems solving, host speakers to discuss algorithms, and provide training sessions for our ICPC teams.
The weekly contest takes place on vjudge.com, where participants are presented with a set of problems to solve within specified time and memory constraints. Solutions can be submitted in various programming languages, including C, C++, Java, and Python, among others. All students from SBU are encouraged to participate. We usually have a collaborative environment where everyone can seek assistance from each other. If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!

name: Breaking Bad Choices
description: Breaking bad...without breaking the law. Join RA Joy and RA Eliot for a Heisenberg-inspired night. Bag blue crystal candy and learn the truth behind common drug myths.

name: Little bugs, big problem
description:
Join RAs Alvin, Bella, and Zarif on learning the environmental impact that lantern flies have. We will understand why their invasion is such a big problem, as well as why it's so important to squash them when you see them. Through a fun kahoot, winners will win a Squishmallow, collectible Micromallow, or a handmade crochet bug keychain! Free snacks provided on a first come first serve basis.

Wednesday October 1st at 8pm in the Tubman MPR.

For any accessibility concerns, please contact isabella.salgado@stonybrook.edu

name: Spicy Ramen Challenge - Yang Hall Council
description:
Join Yang Hall in our Spicy Ramen Challenge!!! Be the fastest to finish your cup without water or drinks and win a prize! Top 3 fastest times will get to choose between a charizard plushie, 6-pack buldak ramen, or sriracha! Everyone is welcome to come and support!

Wednesday, October 8th at 8 pm in the Yang Hall Lounge

Only 18 competitors allowed, so RSVP fast!

name: 2025 Hispanic Heritage Month Opening Celebration
description:
Join us for Stony Brook University’s 36th Annual Hispanic Heritage Month Celebration!

The event kicks off with the Latin American Flags Procession led by Wolfie and the Spirit of Stony Brook Marching Band, followed by cultural performances and interactive activity tables hosted by Latino student clubs. Highlights include a keynote address by Deputy Speaker of the New York State Assembly Phil Ramos and the official Proclamation presented by President Andrea Goldsmith. Celebrate tradition, community, and pride with us!

Co-sponsored by the Hispanic Heritage Month Committee, Diversity, Intercultural and Community Engagement (DICE), Diversity, Inclusion and Intercultural Initiatives (DI3), and Universitarios Unidos Latinoamericanos.

name: Job & Internship Search 101
description:
🚀 Internship Season is HERE! 🚀

Looking for a summer job, internship, or research opportunity? The time to apply is NOW—not next month, not next semester, but right now.

Still stuck scrolling on LinkedIn? 👀 That place is way too crowded. Come learn how to:
✨ Find hidden opportunities on different platforms
✨ Apply to dozens of jobs faster with auto-fill tools (save your sanity!)
✨ Explore options for Engineering, Business, AND Pre-Med students (we’ve got clinical internships + shadowing too!)

This workshop will be fast, practical, and all about making your life easier. 💡


Oh, and if you want us to cover internships specific to your field or dream job, just fill out this quick form 👉

Click here to fill out the RSVP form. https://forms.gle/TFNMatVC1UZsj41D6

A group of survivors of the rage virus lives on a small island. When one of the group leaves the island on a mission into the mainland, he discovers secrets, wonders, and horrors that have mutated not only the infected but other survivors.

Watch the Trailer

name: Carnaval del Barrio
description:
Hispanic Heritage Month is celebrated each year to honor the histories, cultures, and contributions of Americans whose ancestors came from Spain, Mexico, the Caribbean, and Central and South America.

This month-long celebration recognizes the rich traditions, diverse backgrounds, and vibrant cultures that have shaped and continue to enrich the United States

Come and celebrate Hispanic Heritage Month with an event filled with music, dancing, live performances, games and activities, giveaways and food. Honoring Hispanic Heritage month through engaging in the culture, history, food and community.

description: Get into the holiday spirit with Circle K! Join us for a fun and creative evening as we make your very own felt room decor garland, perfect for adding a cozy touch to your space. No crafting experience is needed! Holiday themed goodies will be provided!

name: Turkey Pumpkin and Game Night
description:
Join Gray Hall Council for Turkey Pumpkin & Game Night!
Come craft adorable turkey pumpkins, play board and card games, and enjoy yummy snacks with hot chocolate. Enter our raffle for a chance to win a brand-new keyboard and a LEGO® set.

When: Tuesday, November 12, 7:00–9:00 PM
Where: Gray Hall lower level (basement)
Hosted by: Gray Hall Council • Supported by USG

Bring friends, bring your competitive spirit, and leave with fall vibes (and maybe a prize!).

name: Matcha Cafe
description:
Take a break from the mid-semester stress and join us for a cozy evening at the Match Café! We’ll be serving authentic matcha—a smooth, earthy green tea known for its calming yet energizing properties. Matcha contains L-theanine, an amino acid that promotes relaxation without drowsiness, helping to ease anxiety and improve focus—perfect for students needing a mindful reset.

Enjoy the serene atmosphere with lo-fi beats playing in the background, and settle in to watch a chill movie while sipping your handcrafted matcha latte. Whether you’re here to destress, study in peace, or just vibe with friends, this café-style event is your chance to slow down and recharge.

Details:
📍 Location: Hamilton Classroom
🕖 Time: November 19th, 2025 7:00-9:00
🎶 Featuring: Lo-fi music, authentic matcha, and a relaxing film
👥 Capacity: As supplies last

Come for the tea, stay for the peace.

name: 13th Annual Rainbow Fall Feast
description:
Rainbow Fall Feast is our annual celebration of our LGBTQ* communities at Stony Brook University and of the families that have been found, chosen, and built here. It serves as an opportunity for our students, faculty, staff, and alumni to come together and show thanks for the bonds we've formed during our time here. 

 

This year, we will be expanding our efforts to support the Stony Brook University Seawolves Pantry by collecting donations for the Food Pantry. Any and all non-expired, non-frozen food donations are welcome, but please consider donating one of the high-demand items listed on the Seawolves Pantry website.

 

Donations will be accepted at event check-in, but can also be dropped off at the LGBTQ* Center or the UNITI Cultural Center during hours of operation, beginning Monday, November 10th.

 

For every item you donate, you will receive 1 raffle ticket to enter to win one of several prize baskets, donated by our generous campus partners. You are not required to bring a donation to attend the event, but every donation is greatly appreciated.

 

This year marks our 13th Annual Rainbow Fall Feast! Come join us for a night of great food, a DJ, games, crafts, dancing, and more! 

 

REGISTRATION IS REQUIRED! Seats fill fast, so please reserve your seat here: 13th Annual Rainbow Fall Feast Registration Form

 

We look forward to seeing you! If you have any accessibility needs or concerns, please contact LGBTQ* Services at lgbtqservices@stonybrook.edu or 631-632-1690.

name: Midnight Breakfast
description:
🥞 MIDNIGHT BREAKFAST MADNESS! 🌙
Books down, forks up! Join us for a late-night breakfast feast — because pancakes taste better after dark.

📍 Hand Hall Classroom
🕘 Sunday, December 7, 2025 | 9:00 PM – 11:00 PM

✨ Menu Highlights:
Waffles 🧇 · Toast 🍞 · Fresh Fruit 🍓 · Yogurt 🍦 · Scrambled Eggs 🍳 · Tater Tots 🥔 · Sausage 🌭 · Cereal 🥣

Fuel up, laugh with friends, and kick off finals week with good food and even better company!

description: Join RA Abel at Stress Less Sundae Fest, a relaxing night of creativity and sweet treats! Make your own DIY stress ball to help you unwind, then enjoy a delicious sundae with toppings of your choosing!

Whether you're heading home or staying local, swing by to grab a goodie bag filled with Thanksgiving-themed goodies.

Let’s make this season a little more special, one favor at a time.

name: Mario Kart & Mocktails w/ CPO
description:
🎮🏁 Mario Kart & Mocktails with RA Victoria & CPO! 🏁🎮

Rev your engines and sip something sweet! 

RA Victoria and CPO are bringing you a night of high-speed fun and delicious mocktails! Challenge your friends on the track, laugh until your sides hurt, and pick up some smart tips about staying safe and making responsible choices. 

For accessibility concerns, please email victoria.komenkowski@stonybrook.edu

name: A Lit Preview: Undergraduate Research Opportunities 101
description: Join RAs Angee, Tui, and Isabella as they discuss the nuts and bolts of undergraduate research opportunities in STEM, humanities, and the social sciences. We will talk about where to look for research opportunities, how to prepare for research opportunities, how to manage them on top of schoolwork, and much more!  Donuts will be served.

name: Colloquium Seminar: Wildlife Health and Stress in the Global Change Era: Ecology Meets Epigenomics by Dr. David Daversa
description:
Join us for the next Ecology & Evolution Colloquium on Wednesday, November 19th at 2:15 PM at Life Sciences Building Room 038!

We’re excited to welcome Dr. David Daversa, researcher at the University of California, Los Angeles, as he presents:
"Wildlife Health and Stress in the Global Change Era: Ecology Meets Epigenomics."

name: HOSA Mock-Conference RSVP
description:
HOSA is hosting a Mock-Conference to give you a low-pressure taste of real competition season before the Spring Leadership Conference! 🎓🩺

Join us on Wednesday, December 4th from 11:30 AM–1:30 PM in SAC 303 for HOSA-style competitive events, including HOSA Bowl, Medical/Dental Terminology, Biomedical Debate, and a Spelling Bee. You’ll get to experience how events are run, practice presenting and thinking on your feet, and build confidence for future conferences, interviews, and professional programs.

The event is open to all levels—from complete beginners to experienced competitors—and we encourage business casual attire if possible. Come to test your knowledge, connect with other members and the E-board, and enjoy free food. And yes, you can bring friends, even if they don’t plan to compete in the actual conferences!

name: Diversity Dinner
description: Join Hendrix and Gershwin Hall on November 30th from 7:00 to 9:00 PM in the Hendrix Lounge for a warm and welcoming Diversity Dinner! Enjoy an evening filled with delicious food, meaningful conversations, and a celebration of the cultures that enrich our community. Whether you’re looking to discover new flavors or simply connect with friends and neighbors, we can’t wait to see you there and celebrate the many stories and traditions that make our halls unique!

name: GradConnect Series
description:
We are excited to invite graduate students to the December GradConnect Series (the last one for the Fall semester), organized by the Graduate School.

 

This new monthly series will partner with on-campus and off-campus resources to provide students with direct opportunities to learn about available services and connect. The event will also be a platform for graduate students to interact with each other, share experiences, and build a supportive community.

 

For the December GradConnect Series,  event, we are delighted that the following campus departments and resources are joining to support and connect with graduate students.

  • Visa and Immigration Services (VIS)

Light refreshments will be provided.

 

 Plus, attend at least three of the four Fall 2025 events in the GradConnect Series to be entered into our Grand Raffle Prize drawing in December!  

 

Looking forward to seeing you at the GradConnect Series event.

Don't miss out on the Dodgeball Club's most anticipated event of the year!!! :)

name: Black and Latinx NEW STUDENT Welcome
description:
Black and Latinx New Student Welcome | Wednesday, September 3, 2025 | 7:30 – 9:30 PM | Stony Brook Union Ballroom

Congratulations and welcome to the Seawolf family!

Join us for an evening of fun, friend-making, and community.  This event is aimed at connecting new students to one another and to upperclass students from our Black and Latinx organizations. 

Enjoy a delicious Chicken, Waffles, and Ice Cream Bar while mingling with other new students and hearing from student leaders as they share what they wish they had known when they first started at SBU and the impact cultural organizations can make on your success at Stony Brook.

Plus! Don’t miss out on giveaways and raffle prizes, including:

  • Assorted Games Basket 
  • Seawolves Pride Merch & Swag
  • College Candy & Snacks Basket

Whether you’re here to meet new people, find your community, or just enjoy great food and vibes, you belong here. 
This event is FREE and open to all students.

This event is sponsored by the Office of Diversity, Intercultural and Community Engagement (DICE), in collaboration with the Black Student Union (BSU), the Latin American Student Organization (LASO), and the Black and Latino Initiative for Student Success (BLISS).  

Be the first at our first official cross-collab event of the year.

name: iCafe x iGrad: Tips on How to Do Well on Your Class Presentation
description:
In this workshop, we will review how to prepare, speak confidently, use visuals effectively, and engage your audience. Leave with simple, practical tips to help you succeed in your next class presentation. Lunch will be provided!

Presented by Prof. Haylee Hebenstreit, School of Social Welfare

name: Emerging Talent Networking Dinner
description:
Register on Handshake!

Thursday, October 23, 2025
6pm - 8pm
SAC Ballroom A

Looking to build connections with top employers and explore exciting career opportunities? Join us for the Emerging Talent Networking Dinner, a signature in-person event designed to help you engage directly with professionals from a range of industries who are eager to connect with Stony Brook’s emerging talent.

This unique roundtable dinner format allows you to rotate among employer tables throughout the evening—giving you the chance to introduce yourself, ask questions, and gain insights into a variety of companies, roles, and career paths. Whether you're exploring internships, part-time roles, or full-time positions, this is a great opportunity to expand your network and make a memorable impression.

Event Highlights:

  • Enjoy dinner while participating in small-group conversations with professionals

  • Rotate tables to maximize your exposure to different employers

  • Hear from a keynote speaker who will share career inspiration and insight

  • Learn about current openings and career pathways directly from company reps

  • Engage with campus student organizations co-hosting the event

  • Wrap up with open networking alongside students, alumni, and employers

 

Come prepared with questions, your resume, and your curiosity—this is your chance to shine in a professional yet approachable setting.

name: Dreiser Hall Trivia Night
description:
In this event, attendees will compete in a trivia game (Kahoot) to correctly answer as many possible questions about Dreiser Hall! This event is a great way to meet new people and learn more about your new home for the academic year! Snacks and candy will be provided.

For questions and more information, please contact RA Joey at joseph.litjens@stonybrook.edu

name: Be Well Fest
description:
Get Ready for the Third Annual Be Well Fest!
Join us in celebrating the return of the Be Well Fest. The perfect way to kick off the semester at our Student Health and Counseling Center! Enjoy outdoor activities, grab exciting giveaways, savor freshly popped popcorn, and cool off with free ice cream.

Step inside to explore our health and counseling spaces, where you can meet the friendly staff from Student Health Services (SHS) and Counseling and Psychological Services (CAPS). Everyone who signs up for a tour will receive limited vouchers for on-campus Dunkin’ or Starbucks. A talented DJ will set the mood with your favorite tunes, and outdoor games like cornhole will be available, so bring your game face! As a special creative touch, you can also participate in tie-dyeing activities led by the Center for Prevention and Outreach (CPO) team.

Stay healthy this semester by getting your flu vaccine at the first flu vaccine pod of the fall. The flu vaccine is one of the most effective ways to protect yourself and contribute to a healthier campus community.

The first 200 students to complete a building tour will receive a free voucher for Starbucks or Dunkin’ Donuts! Tours will be held every 20 minutes, starting at 12:00 PM, with the final tour at 2:40 PM.

Mark your calendars for this exciting day of community, connection, and celebration. We can't wait to see you there!

name: National Voter Education Week:  Make a Plan
description:
National Voter Education Week is a nonpartisan initiative aimed at enhancing voter engagement through collaboration with colleges, universities, corporations, and election officials. The week focuses on all aspects of voting, including voter registration and turnout. Students will be educated on various topics, such as how to complete voter registration forms, request absentee ballots, and more. Join us on Wednesday to make a plan to vote in the 2025 election. 

 

* Time and Location are TBD*

name: Heat & Heat: Fire Safety and Prevention
description:
Fire prevention at Stony Brook University is a year-round effort by Fire and Emergency Services. During the month of September, we conduct numerous fire safety lectures, fire extinguisher training, and live-burn demonstrations in each of the residential communities and host an annual fire and safety expo on the academic mall. 

Join us and the first 300 participants will get a voucher for food and refreshment for the event.

name: FCC Peanut Butter Noodle Night
description: Get ready for one of the Fujianese Culture Club's signature traditions: PB Noodle Night! Centered around the classic Fujianese dish, ban mian (peanut butter noodles), this event brings together the beautiful culture surrounding this dish. Come enjoy a bowl of rich, flavorful noodles while diving into interactive games and activities inspired by peanut butter noodles. Whether you’re here to eat, play, or learn, PB Noodle Night is the perfect chance to experience the taste of Fujianese culture and connect with FCC.

name: KSA x TSA Presents: Lunar Lounge
description: Stony Brook KSA x TSA presents mocktails teaching about drinking etiquette and a safe drinking culture along with small foods that pair well with the mocktails provided.

name: Join RA Kloey to the Center For Civic Justice's Community Dialouge: LGBT and Local Government
description: Join RA Kloey for a night of discussion, learning, and belonging at the Communtiy Dialouge hosted by the Center for Civic Justice on the topic of LGBT and Local Government. Dinner will be provided and inspiring conversations will be had!

name: CNA:Mid-Autumn Hanfu Cultural Event
description: This event is organized to celebrate the Mid-Autumn Festival by inviting students to wear Hanfu which is a traditional Chinese clothing. Participants will engage in small games and cultural understanding.Light festive snacks will be provided. The program aims to create a welcoming environment for students to connect, share tradition, and experience the cultural significance of the Mid-Autumn Festival together. beginning at 6.30pm, we will be likely to check in Participants at the Sac Lobby, the event will be expected to start at 7pm.

name: Hidden Treasures: Sustainable Jeopardy
description: Test your knowledge and compete with friends at Hidden Treasures’ Sustainable Jeopardy! Inspired by the classic game show, this interactive event will challenge you with fun, thought-provoking questions on topics like eco-friendly habits and waste reduction. The winning group will win an eco-friendly prize 🌱🏆
